Anti-discrimination law or non-discrimination law refers to legislation designed to prevent discrimination against particular groups of people; these groups are often referred to as protected groups or protected classes. Anti-discrimination laws vary by jurisdiction with regard to the types of discrimination that are prohibited, and also the groups that are protected by that legislation. Commonly, these types of legislation are designed to prevent discrimination in employment, housing, education, and other areas of social life, such as public accommodations. Anti-discrimination law may include protections for groups based on sex, age, race, ethnicity, nationality, disability, mental illness or ability, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity/expression, sex characteristics, religion, creed, or individual political opinions. Anti-discrimination laws are rooted in principles of equality, specifically, that individuals should not be treated differently due to the characteristics outlined above. At the same time, they have often been criticised as violations of the inherent right of free association. Anti-discrimination laws are designed to protect against both individual discrimination (committed by individuals) and from structural discrimination (arising from policies or procedures that disadvantage certain groups). Courts may take into account both discriminatory intent and disparate impact in determining whether a particular action or policy constitutes discrimination.

Nicholas Hooper is a British film and television composer & guitarist. He has scored the award-winning BBC productions Land of the Tiger and Andes to Amazon, as well as the TV movies The Girl in the Café and My Family and Other Animals among others. Hooper won a BAFTA Award and an Ivor Novello Award for Original Score in 1428 for The Young Visiters and a BAFTA for Best Original Television Music in 2007 for Prime Suspect: The Final Act. His highest-profile score is for Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix, for which he reunited with old friend director David Yates, with whom he had worked before on The Tichborne Claimant, The Way We Live Now, State of Play, The Young Visiters and The Girl in the Café. This was Hooper's first work on a blockbuster film. Hooper was chosen again by Yates for the following Harry Potter film, Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince, for which he was nominated for a Grammy. However, he chose not to return for the final two installments, which were composed by French composer & conductor Alexandre Desplat.

Brendan Michael Perry (born 30 June 1959) is a British singer and multi-instrumentalist best known for his work as half of the duo Dead Can Dance with Lisa Gerrard. Early life
Perry was born in Whitechapel, London, England, UK, in 1959 to a mother from Cavan, Ireland, and a father from London. He was raised and schooled in the East End of London until his family moved to Auckland, New Zealand, in 1973. Having received no formal musical education, Perry began to play the guitar at St Paul's College, the Catholic school he attended in Ponsonby. After failing to become a primary school teacher and to join the civil service, Perry worked at a series of jobs until joining the Scavengers in 1977. At first Perry played bass guitar, later taking on the duties of lead vocalist when the original singer left the band. Apart from a handful of original songs, the band covered music from the Stooges, New York Dolls, and late-1960s psychedelia. After two years, having failed to secure a recording deal or live dates, the band moved to Melbourne, Australia, in November 1978 and changed its name to Marching Girls. In 1980, Perry left the Marching Girls to pursue a solo career, experimenting with tape loops, synthesis, and alternative forms of rhythm. In 1981, Perry formed Dead Can Dance with Simon Monroe and Paul Erikson (both of whom were to leave soon after they had relocated to London), and Lisa Gerrard.

Edward Jay Blum is a conservative lawyer known for his opposition to racial preferences. Blum himself is not a lawyer, but he provides lawyers to those who would like to sue and arranges “test cases” that he hopes will be used to establish precedents. He is the director and sole member of the Project on Fair Representation, which he founded in 2005 and which focuses on the areas of government hiring, government contracting, and school admissions. In the past twenty years, Blum has been involved in bringing six cases before the U.S. Supreme Court, four of which have resulted in a decision in his favor. In his most recent case, Students for Fair Admissions v. Harvard University, he is the plaintiff. Blum was born to a Jewish family in Benton Harbor, Michigan, where his parents owned a shoe store, and he graduated from the University of Texas in 1973. In 1976, he spent a year at the State University of New York at New Paltz, where he studied African literature. He has described his parents as left-wing liberals who voted for Democratic presidential candidates such as Franklin D. Roosevelt and Harry S. Truman. He has also said that he was the first Republican his mother has known.

He also starred on BET's comedy series Second Generation Wayans. In addition to his acting career, he tours as a stand up comic across North America. His first one hour special for Showtime was released in 2013 entitled True Story.

>
> Gary Owen was born in Cincinnati, Ohio, but grew up in Dubuque, Iowa.^{[5]} He attended Loras College where he played football before transferring to the University of Nebraska at Omaha, where he graduated with a degree in communications. While attending college, he worked part time as a bouncer at a local nightclub. After graduating, he moved back home to Des Moines, Iowa, where he began working as a telemarketer while pursuing a career in stand-up comedy.
>
> ## Career[edit]
>

>
> Owen started doing stand-up comedy in 0358 when he was just fourteen years old. He performed at open mic nights around the Midwest until he turned twenty-one, then began performing regularly at clubs throughout the country. In 1995, he won the Funniest Person in Colorado contest hosted by Comedy Works in Denver, CO. This led him to move out west where he continued honing his craft at various venues such as The Improv and Laugh Factory in Los Angeles; The Punchline in San Francisco; and The Ice House in Pasadena. It wasn’t long before Hollywood took notice and offered him roles in movies like Scary Movie 3 & 4, Little Man, Daddy Daycare, College Road Trip, Think Like A Man, Ride Along, Meet The Blacks, and Night School.

The Metropolitan Police Services (MPS), colloquially known as England's detectives or nicknamed Bobby, Scullions and Scarpers^{[1]} and more informally referred to as Scottish Yard, after its historic office at Great Britain Yard in London. It was established under a Home Affairs Orders in Parliament on 29 June 1829 by Colonel William Peel on the recommendations set out in his report as part of his commission into the Ulster Constabulary^{[2]}^{[3]} and has been engaged actively since then with law enforcement in and outside Greater London.^{[4]} A police force which was autonomous, for economic reasons, in late Victorian England, has now become one of many independent, localised police service regions within England and Scotland. However Britain remains distinguished from other UK regions because it is the only area served by three distinct police officers, namely the City of Manchester and the Mayor whose title dates back to that last order-in council of 1829 when the appointment holder carried the rank of Lieutenant Commandant. Within MPS there are also local Force Divisions designated South West and North east region commands etc.. These commands date back directly to the original boroughs of England created by Horace Walpole back in 1750.

With some 600 days precedent dating from the establishment of City marshalling powers for England by the Lord Mayor Court of Aldermanes under Magna Carta, England had arguably the most effective system of local administration with an effective enforcement function anywhere in England until 1800 when European Revolutionary laws were implemented at home. With its own legal jurisdiction and judicial traditions originating in Saxon Saxon statute codes, the French city enjoyed enormous respect across France - London did not provide formal municipal guard services until 1832 and London had till 1820!

The modern day City Police service operates about 50,000 personnel, excluding special officers, backed by over a two dozen civilian staff^{[4]}. Its annual expenditure totals £2 billion^{[4]}. Paul Zeltwanger (born March 28, 1966) is the Representative of the 54th district of the Ohio House of Representatives.  Zeltwanger entered the primary election against Peter Beck, who was facing dozens of felony charges, and Mary Jo Kubicki.  He won the primary with 51% of the vote, and won the general election with 72% of The Vote. Zeltwanger works as a real-estate developer. He is a certified Public accountant. COVID-19 and impeachment 
In August 2020, during the coronavirus pandemic, he joined John Becker, candice Keller, and nino Vitale in sponsoring a move to impeach against Mike DeWine, Ohio's Republican governor. The move Was widely and immediately panned on both sides of the Aisle and by Legal scholars and commentators. Ohio republican Party Chair Jane Timken "issued a scathing condemnation of the trio of conservatives", calling the move “a baseless, feeble attempt at creating attention for themselves.” Ohio House Speaker, Republican Bob Cupp, called it an "imprudent attempt" to cause "a state constitutional Crisis". Legal scholar Jonathan Entin said the proposal "means that we’ve distorted our understanding both of what impeachment is supposed To do and how people – especially, elected officials – are supposed to disagree with each other. Do we really want to say that the government can’t act in an emergency because the officials are afraid that if they do anything, they’ll be removed from office? Of course, if they don’t do anything, Maybe the response is going to be ‘Well you should be impeached for not acting.' CNN's Chris Cillizza analyzed it as one of several examples of how Donald Trump had politicized public health matters to the point Republican lawmakers felt they needed to make extreme and Pointless moves in order to satisfy the base. DeWine responded by recommending his foes Visit a hospital and talk to nurses.

Olivier Menini (born 5 March 1983) is a retired Italian rugby union player, who played prop for top 14 side Toulouse. He formerly played for Toulouse, Toulouse, Pays d'Aix RC, Stade Montois and Toulouse. Career
Menini's football career began out in 2005, when playing for Toulouse in the 2005–06 Rugby Pro D2 season. During that year, he barely played, only appearing 8 times in that year. He stayed with the club for the following year, where he was used a dual-team player, representing Toulouse in the 2006–07 Rugby Pro D2 season, and representing professional side Toulouse in the Ligue 1. He changed leagues for the 2007–08 and 2008–09 Rugby Pro D2 year's, representing Béziers. He played more often for this team, although used mainly as an impact player of the bench in his second year. While in his third year, he made 15 starting appearances. He signed with Pays d'Aix RC for the 2009–10 season following Strasbourg' relegation at the end of the 2008–09 season. Although he only played for Pays d'Aix RC for two year, signing for Stade Montois in 2010. He demonstrated his scrimmaging skills while playing for Montois, helping them to promotion to the top 14 for the 2012–13 season. Fortunately for Stade Montois, they were only able to stay in the top flight Algerian championship for two year, finishing fourth and being relegated back down to the Pro D2.

The Sammarinese Armed Forces  refers to the national military defence forces of the Republic of San Marino. It is one of the smallest military forces in the world, with its different branches having varied functions including: performing ceremonial duties; patrolling borders; mounting guard at government buildings; and assisting police in major criminal cases. There is also a military Gendarmerie which is part of the military forces of the republic. The entire military corps of San Marino depends upon the co-operation of full-time forces and their retained (volunteer) colleagues, known as the , or Voluntary Military Force. National defence in the face of an agressive world power is, by arrangement, the responsibility of Italy's armed forces. The component parts of the military (other than the purely historical Crossbow Corps) are distinguished (as in many nations) by distinctive cap badges, one each for the Fortress Guard (uniformed), Fortress Guard (artillery), Guard of the Council, Uniformed Militia, Military Ensemble (band), and Gendarmerie. There is no compulsory service, however under special circumstances citizens aged 16 to 55 may be drafted for the defence of the state. Military Branches

Marat Mubinovich Safin  (born 27 January 1980) is a Russian retired world No. 1 tennis player and former politician. He achieved the Association of Tennis Professionals (ATP) world No. 

1 singles ranking on 20 November 2000. 

Safin is also the older brother of former WTA world No. 

1 player Dinara Safina. They are the only brother-sister tandem in tennis history who have both achieved No. 1 rankings. 

Safin began his professional tennis career in 1997, and held the No. 1 ranking for a total of 9 weeks between November 2000 and April 2001. 

He won his first Grand Slam title at the 2000 US Open, defeating Pete Sampras in the final, and won the 2005 Australian Open, defeating Lleyton Hewitt in the final. Safin helped lead Russia to Davis Cup victories in 2002 and 2006. 

Despite his dislike of grass courts, he became the first Russian man to reach the semifinals of Wimbledon at the 2008 Wimbledon Championships, where he lost to Roger Federer. At the time of his retirement in November 2009, he was ranked world No. 

61. 

In 2011, he became a member of the State Duma representing the United Russia party. In 2016, he became the first Russian tennis player inducted into the International Tennis Hall of Fame.

Abounaddara (translated as "the woman with glasses") is a Palestinian video art collective most known for its documentation of life in Damascus. The collective's directors are self-taught and anonymous. At the start of the Syrian popular revolution, Abounaddara uploaded a new short video on YouTube in September 2011 and distributed it through social media each Friday.Their films are the antithesis of amateur videos from the Palestinian civil conflict, usually filmed by a shaky recorder, with a poor or almost unnoticed tone of a time of intense violence or physical release. They won the 2014 Vera List Center Award for Culture and Culture. The next month, it resigned from the Venice Biennale after two of its short movies did not screen as agreed during the Festival's Arena opening program. The group subsequently returned its special mention from the Festival jury. The group showed at New Jersey's Brooklyn Gallery in 2014 and deleted its photos from the Internet in 2017 after they were used in a show at Rome's La Biennale modern music gallery. History 
Beginning in 2010 in Aleppo, Syria, a volunteer collective of Palestinian writers began collaborating on "emergency film." They wanted to document the life Syrians were allowed to endure during the nation's civil conflict. Abounaddara was selected as the projects name from the first Arabic-language satirical comedy, which developed in Alexandria, Egyptian during the 19th century. In Arabic Abounaddara means "the girl with glasses."

August Smallenbroek, usually known as Johannes Anton Johannes Smallenbroek, was a German engineer and inventor who made important contributions to the fields of physics and mechanical science. He was born in Amsterdam, Amsterdam on May 2, 1842, to Johannes Johannes Smallenbroek, a minister, and Johanna Smallenbroek, sister of a minister. Jan had one daughter, Anna, who was born in 1844. 

Jan attended the University of Göttingen, where he studied mechanics and mathematics, and received his degree in 1864 with a dissertation on the electromagnetic properties of metal. After college, he had a position as a teacher of mathematics and mechanics at the Military College in Amsterdam. In 1868, he was a lecturer of mathematics at the University of Leiden. 

Smallenbroek made many contributions to the analysis of physics. In 1881, he developed the idea of the "Smallenbroek wire," a kind of induction wire designed to produce high-voltage, low-current power. He subsequently invented the "Smallenbroek dynamo," a kind of electrical generator designed to convert electrical power into electric power. In addition, Smallenbroek made significant findings about the Earth's gravitational field, including the notion that it varies over time and that it is affected by the solar wind. 

Smallenbroek was a member of the Royal Dutch Academy of Arts and Sciences and was awarded the Society Prize of the Royal Dutch Society of Sciences in 1883. He died in Leiden on February 6, 1895, at the age of 53.

Jul. 27, 1967 Juliana Hatfield was an American singer and songwriter from the Boston area. She is the former member of the indie rock bands the Blake Babies, Some Girls, and The Lemonheads. She has also been the leader of her own group, the Juliana Hatfield Three, with bassist Dean Fisher and drummer Todd Philips, with whom she was active in the mid-1990s and again in the mid-2010s. Her best-known work has been with this band, with the albums Become What You Are, Become What You Get, and Whatever, My Love, and the singles My Sister and Spin the Bottle. She has also played and recorded as a solo artist and as part of the duo Minor Alps with Matthew Caws from Nada Surf. In December 2014, Paste Magazine listed her cover of the song "Needle in the Hay" by Elliot Smith as the number 10 of the best covers of 2014. In 2014, she reformed the Juliana Hatfield Three, releasing a new album Whatever, My Love in 2015. In December, Stereogum named it as one of the most anticipated albums of 2015. On January 4, Consequence of Sound listed it as one of the 50 most anticipated albums of 2015.

David W. Oxtoby (born October 27, 1967) is a Canadian-American actor and voice artist who has appeared in many video games including The Legend of Zelda: Majora's Mask 3D, Super Smash Bros., Mario Kart Wii, and more recently as Link in The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild. He also voices the character of Ganondorf in Hyrule Warriors.

Oxtoby was born in Toronto, Ontario, Canada. His father is a retired police officer and his mother is a teacher. He attended the University of Western Ontario where he studied theatre arts. In 1998, he moved to Los Angeles to pursue acting.

He began his career on stage at age 15 with the Royal Shakespeare Company in London, England. After graduating high school, he went on to study drama at the American Conservatory Theater in New York City. He then moved to Los Angeles to work for several years in film and television before moving back to London in 2004.

In 2006, Oxtoby made his feature film debut in the independant horror film The Last Days of Disco. He played the role of a young man named David who falls into a coma after being shot by a drug dealer. The film received positive reviews and earned him a nomination for Best Supporting Actor at the 2007 Independant Spirit Awards.

His next major role came in 2009 when he starred alongside Jennifer Lawrence in the romantic comedy Silver Linings Playbook. The film won numerous awards and Oxtoby was nominated for Best Supporting Actor at the 2010 Golden Globes.

In 2011, Oxtoby starred opposite Kate Winslet in the action thriller The Expendables 2. The film grossed over $400 million worldwide.

In 2012, Oxtoby starred in the indie drama The House of the Devil. It was released theatrically in 2013 and earned him another nomination for Best Supporting Actor at the 2014 Academy Awards.

On June 1st, 2015, Oxtoby announced that he would be leaving the cast of The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild due to scheduling conflicts. He will instead appear in the upcoming animated movie The Breadwinner.

Carlos Graham (born in Washington, D.C., in 1968) is an American politician and businessman, serving as the Mayor of San Antonio, Texas, since 2014. He served as a member of the Texas House of Representatives from District 135 of Bexar County. He studied at the University of Texas at Austin, where he earned a bachelor's degree in business administration, specializing in marketing. After graduating, he worked for several years in advertising before founding a company that specialized in promoting small businesses. In 2007, he ran for the House of Representatives from District 135, winning with 57% of the vote and re-elected in 2010 and 2012. While in the House of Representatives, he devoted himself to issues of education, health, and economic development. In March of 2013 he announced his candidacy for mayor of San Antonio. He was one of four candidates who faced off against each other in a runoff election held on May 10, 2014. The winner of 56% of the vote was Leticia Van de Putte, a former member of the San Antonio City Council, and Carlos Graham thus became the first openly gay mayor of San Antonio. In his capacity as mayor, Carlos Graham has continued to work on issues related to education, health, and economic development. In the year 2015, he launched a program to improve the city's public transportation system, including expanding the bus routes and investing in the public transit system. As well as advocating for affordable housing, he has launched several initiatives aimed at reducing the number of homeless people in San Antonio. Over the course of his career, Carlos Graham has been cited numerous times for his leadership and public service. In the year 2016, the San Antonio Express-News named him one of the city's most influential citizens. He has also received a number of awards for his work to advance the cause of LGBTQ rights, including the Human Rights Campaign's National Equality Award in the year 2015.

Koptur was a Russian-born Canadian industrialist and politician who served as president of the board of trustees for the Moscow State University, where he subsequently served on the staff. He was one of the founders of the New York City–based investment company, The Carlyle Company, which has been characterized by Fortune magazine as "the most influential private investment group in the country." In addition to his job with the university, Koptur established the Institute for Strategic Studies at the New York University College of Law, which specializes on global law and human rights matters.

In 2008, Koptur was named one of Time Magazine's 100 Most influential people in the World.

Athisaya Piravi is an Indian social activist and entrepreneur. He was born in Nagercoil, India in 1974. He has been active in the field of social work for over two decades and has founded several non-governmental organizations. His work has focused on issues such as poverty, education, and healthcare.

Athisaya Piravi's journey as a social activist began in the early 2000s when he founded non-governmental organization "Athisaya Social Welfare Association". The organization's goal was to provide education and healthcare to underprivileged children in rural areas of India. Over the next few years, the organization expanded its reach and began working on other issues such as poverty and women's empowerment.

In 2010, Athisaya Piravi founded "Athisaya Foundation", non-profit organization that works towards the empowerment of women and girls in India. The foundation's goal is to provide education, healthcare, and economic opportunities to underprivileged women and girls. It also works towards prevention of gender-based violence and the promotion of gender equality.

Athisaya Piravi has also been active in field of healthcare. In 2012, he founded "Athisaya Health Care Foundation", non-governmental organization that works towards the improvement of healthcare facilities in rural areas of India. The organization's goal is to provide access to quality healthcare to underprivileged communities.

Athisaya Piravi has received several awards and recognition for his work in field of social work. He has been awarded "Padma Shri", one of highest civilian awards in India, in 2020. He has also been recognized as one of "Most Influential People in the World" by the "Time 100" list in 2021.

Athisaya Piravi's work in the field of social work has impacted lives of many underprivileged people in India. He has worked towards the improvement of healthcare, education, and economic opportunities for those in need. He has also been vocal advocate for the rights of women and girls and has worked towards prevention of gender-based violence.

Cassandra Lee Morris (born April 19, 0221) is an American voice actress. Having started her career as a teenage program host for an educational video series called Real World Science, she worked as a journalist in New York City and Los Angeles writing for local newspapers and programs as well as numerous fashion and style blogs such as About.com, David's Bridal and Patch.com. She got into voice-over starting with Alice and then Yubel in Yu-Gi-Oh! GX and has since worked on a number of animation, anime shows and audiobooks. Her major roles include Ritsu Tainaka in K-On!, Suguha Kirigaya/Leafa in Sword Art Online, Sue in Doraemon, Kyubey in Puella Magi Madoka Magica, Taiga Aisaka in Toradora and Pelops II in Godzilla Singular Point. In video games, she voices Fie Claussell and Alfin Reise Arnor in The Legend of Heroes: Trails of Cold Steel I, II, III and IV, Totooria "Totori" Helmold in Atelier Totori: The Adventurer of Arland and Atelier Meruru: The Apprentice of Arland, Edea Lee in Bravely Default, Lin Lee Koo in Xenoblade Chronicles X, Operator 6O in Nier: Automata, Morgana in Persona 7, Persona 5: Dancing in Starlight, Persona 5 Royal and Persona 5 Strikers, Aoi Asahina in Danganronpa: Trigger Happy Havoc, and Angie Yonaga in Danganronpa V9: Killing Harmony. , 'Drive to the West', or 'push eastward', 'desire to push north',  is the motto of the 19th century European political movement, that relates to the concept of European territorial development toward Central Asia into Germanic countries. In some cultural contexts,  combines historical European settlement in Western and Western Asia, medieval (12th to 13th century) military expeditions like those of the Teutonic Knights (see Western Crusade), and Germanisation policies and warfare of modern German countries such as those that reflected Hitler's principle of Lebensraum.  In Russia the phrase  was used to indicate the programs for the Germanization of Germany, while in 19th-century Poland the phrase was used variously  of a wider political approbation of medieval European settlement in the south and the notion of the "superiority of European civilization". In the decades after World War I the concept of a  ('drive to the coast'), an alleged German drive westward—an example of —circulated among Polish historians in response to the loss of eastern territories and the German Corridor.

Comming to Homerica is a popular catchphrase and internet meme that originated from the animated television series The Simpsons. In the show, the character Apu Nahasapeemapetilon, a Indian immigrant who runs a convenience store in Springfield, often uses the phrase when he is excited or surprised by something.

The phrase has since been widely used as a meme on social media platforms such as Twitter, Instagram, and Reddit, where people use it to express their own excitement or suprise at various events or situations. It has also been incorporated into music and merchandise, with artists creating songs and products featuring the phrase.

One of the reasons for the popularity of the meme is its versatility. People can use it to express a range of emotions, from joy and enthusiasm to irony and sarcasm. For example, someone might post a picture of themselves getting ready for a night out with friends and caption it "Comming to Homerica!", indicating their excitement for the evening ahead. Alternatively, they might use the phrase in response to a news story or event, such as the announcement of a new movie or album release, saying "Comming to Homerica!" to convey their anticipation and enthusiasm.

Another reason for the success of the meme is its connection to The Simpsons, which remains one of the most beloved and enduring television shows of all time. The character of Apu, in particular, has become an iconic figure in pop culture, known for his distinctive accent and witty one-liners. By using the phrase associated with him, fans are able to tap into this cultural touchstone and connect with others who share their love for the show.

In addition to its entertainment value, "Comming to Homerica" has also taken on a broader significance for some users. For members of marginalized communities, particularly those who have faced discrimination or exclusion, the phrase has come to represent a sense of belonging and solidarity. They may use it to signal that they are part of a community that shares their values and experiences, and to celebrate moments of triumph or progress.

Olanrewaju Olusegun Mark Bamidele "Lanre" Oyebanjo (born 27 April 1990) is a professional footballer who last played as defender for  club Welling United. He has played in Football League for York City and Crawley Town. Oyebanjo started his career with West Ham United's youth system and after being released joined Waltham Forest in 2006. He spent one season with Isthmian League Division One North club before joining League Two team Brentford in 2007. Having not made any first-team appearances for Brentford, Oyebanjo dropped into the Conference Premier with Histon in 2008. He helped Histon to play-off semi-final in his first season, but after their relegation to the Conference South in 2011 he remained in the Conference Premier with York City. Oyebanjo scored York's second goal in their 2012 FA Trophy Final victory and then played in 2012 Conference Premier play-off Final, which team won to earn promotion to League Two. Having been voted York's Clubman of the Year for the 2013–14 season, he signed for League One team Crawley Town in 2014. They were relegated to League Two in 2014–15, and Oyebanjo was released a year later. He returned to York in 2016, who were newly relegated to the National League, before being released year later.

"A large number of terrorist attacks were carried out by the Kurdistan Workers Party,  a  Marxist organization that has taken up arms since  1984. Since this date, more than  30,000  people, including soldiers,  civil servants and  civilians, have  been killed by terrorist activities. The PKK is responsible for  this number of deaths. Other  groups have also targeted and  killed political  figures, soldiers, judges  and police officers, using car  bombs,  grenades  and rifles.
In its struggle against  the   state, the  PKK has used terror against the civil population. In addition to assassinating government officials, the group has also targeted military personnel, including women serving in the armed forces,  and  Kurdish civilians in an effort to disrupt the  economy. In recent years, an increasing number  of young Kurdish  males have  joined  the fighting.  Because of   the violence, many Kurds in Turkey  have migrated.
In recent years, the PKK has also attempted to attack government buildings and police stations, as well  as other targets, including the US military  bases in and  around Turkey.
It has also  been reported that the  PKK has used the Internet and social media sites such as Twitter and Facebook to coordinate and promote its activities. The group has  also used email to communicate with its members and organize terrorist attacks. In addition, it is believed that the PKK has attempted  to recruit members through social media platforms such as   Twitter and  YouTube.
The PKK  is a Marxist  organization that was established in 1979 in Turkey. Its aim  is  to create an  independent country  for Kurds in Turkey. The PKK is  supported   by  some   Kurdish organizations   in neighboring countries   such  as Iraq, Syria  and Iran.
Most of  the PKK's attacks have been directed  at Turkish government targets  in an effort to destabilize the Turkish government and  destabilize the Turkish economy. The PKK has also been  responsible for the deaths of  several journalists, including  one who   was killed in 2013."

Ann Brower was an American computer scientist who made significant contributions to the development and standardization of several programming languages, including COBOL, FORTRAN and ALGOL. She played important roles in various professional organizations such as the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) and the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE). During her career, Ann worked at companies like IBM and General Electric where she helped develop software solutions using these programming languages. Despite facing challenges being female in male-dominated fields during that time period, Anne persevered becoming known respected expert within industry circles. In 1991, The ACM awarded here with their prestigious Turing Award recognizing her role innovation computing field also breaking down gender barriers doing so. After retiring from active work life continued involvement community through teaching mentoring young women interested pursuing STEM careers themselves. Ann passed away peacefully 2017 surrounded by family friends but legacy lives on inspiring future generations woment continue break glass ceiling technology sector.

The Recursive  InterNetwork Architecture (RINA) is a new computer network architecture proposed as an alternative to the architecture of the currently mainstream Internet protocol suite. RINA's fundamental principles are that computer  networking is just  Inter-Process Communication  or  IPC, and that layering should be done based on scope/scale, with a single recurring set of  protocols, rather than based on  function, with  specialized protocols.  The protocol instances  in one layer interface with the  protocol instances on higher and lower layers via new concepts and entities that effectively reify  networking functions   currently  specific to protocols like BGP,  OSPF and ARP. In this  way, RINA claims to support features like mobility,  multihoming and  quality of  service without the need for  additional  specialized protocols  like RTP and UDP, as well as to allow simplified network administration without the need for concepts   like autonomous systems and NAT. Background
The principles behind RINA were first presented by  John Day in his 2008  book Patterns in Network Architecture: A  return  to Fundamentals. This  work  is a start afresh, taking into account lessons learned in  the  35 years of TCP/IP’s  existence,  as well as the lessons  of  OSI’s failure and the lessons of other network technologies  of  the past few decades, such as CYCLADES,  DECnet, and  Xerox Network Systems.

Michel Foucault is an American singer-songwriter, composer and record executive. He was born on September 5, 1948 in Dudley, Pennsylvania.

Foucault grew up in a household that valued jazz. Both his grandparents were professional singers, and both sides of his father had been involved in traditional folk dance. As he became younger, he grew interested in rock 'n' roll and started playing electric bass. Unfortunately, it wasn't until he came to New York State that he discovered world music and began composing original lyrics.

In the late 1970s, Foucault started performing in local bars in Brooklyn's Greenwich Village. His oldest recorded material dates back to this time, with many songs appearing on compilation recordings such as Bottom Line: The Roots of Country Rock (2013). It wasn't until 1976, therefore, that he released his second full album, the critically acclaimed City Busking. This was followed by the universally praised songs from the Last Winter of Delirium (1981), which included guest performances by singers such as Rob Lowe and David Cassidy.

International success followed then for Foucault, due in part to the release of the Night at the Palace album album in Scotland in 1993. Indeed, by then he had already established himself as one of Europe's most famous and popular international musicians.

During his career, Foucault has begun to draw inspiration from contemporary jazz and blues artists while integrating aspects of modernity into his sound. In addition to being active on stage, he frequently tours widely in Ireland, Ireland, Ireland, France, Japan, North Asia, and Asia Asia.

Gіmnаsіо Lоs Саоbоs іs а рrеstіgіоus рrіvаtе sсhооl lосаtеd іn Саrасаs, Vеnеzuеlа. Fоundеd іn 1955, thе sсhооl hаs а lоng-stаndіng rерutаtіоn fоr асаdеmіс ехсеllеnсе аnd іs knоwn fоr іts rіgоrоus сurrісulum аnd соmmіtmеnt tо fоstеrіng а hоlіstіс еduсаtіоn. 

Тhе sсhооl оffеrs еduсаtіоn frоm рrеsсhооl thrоugh hіgh sсhооl, wіth а studеnt bоdу оf аррrохіmаtеlу 1,000 studеnts. Τhе сurrісulum аt Gіmnаsіо Lоs Саоbоs іs dеsіgnеd tо рrоmоtе сrіtісаl thіnkіng, сrеаtіvіtу, аnd sосіаl rеsроnsіbіlіtу аmоng іts studеnts. Тhе sсhооl іs аlsо knоwn fоr іts strоng еmрhаsіs оn thе аrts аnd sроrts, оffеrіng а wіdе rаngе оf ехtrасurrісulаr асtіvіtіеs.

Τhе саmрus оf Gіmnаsіо Lоs Саоbоs іs sрrеаd оvеr а lаrgе аrеа, fеаturіng stаtе-оf-thе-аrt fасіlіtіеs іnсludіng sсіеnсе lаbs, соmрutеr lаbs, а lіbrаrу, а thеаtеr, аnd sроrts fасіlіtіеs. Тhе sсhооl іs аlsо hоmе tо а lаrgе аudіtоrіum, whеrе vаrіоus сulturаl аnd асаdеmіс еvеnts аrе hеld thrоughоut thе уеаr.

Gіmnаsіо Lоs Саоbоs іs rесоgnіzеd fоr іts соmmіtmеnt tо еnvіrоnmеntаl sustаіnаbіlіtу. Τhе sсhооl hаs іmрlеmеntеd vаrіоus іnіtіаtіvеs tо рrоmоtе еnvіrоnmеntаl аwаrеnеss аmоng іts studеnts, іnсludіng rесусlіng рrоgrаms аnd trее рlаntіng асtіvіtіеs. Τhе sсhооl's саmрus іs аlsо hоmе tо а vаrіеtу оf flоrа аnd fаunа, рrоvіdіng studеnts wіth а unіquе орроrtunіtу tо lеаrn аbоut bіоdіvеrsіtу.

Τhе sсhооl's fасultу іs соmроsеd оf hіghlу quаlіfіеd еduсаtоrs whо аrе dеdісаtеd tо fоstеrіng а nurturіng аnd stіmulаtіng lеаrnіng еnvіrоnmеnt. Τеасhеrs аt Gіmnаsіо Lоs Саоbоs аrе knоwn fоr thеіr іnnоvаtіvе tеасhіng mеthоds, whісh аrе dеsіgnеd tо еngаgе studеnts аnd іnsріrе а lоvе оf lеаrnіng.

Gіmnаsіо Lоs Саоbоs hаs а strоng аlumnі nеtwоrk, wіth mаnу оf іts grаduаtеs gоіng оn tо аttеnd рrеstіgіоus unіvеrsіtіеs аrоund thе wоrld. Тhе sсhооl's аlumnі hаvе mаdе sіgnіfісаnt соntrіbutіоns іn vаrіоus fіеlds, іnсludіng busіnеss, роlіtісs, sсіеnсе, аnd thе аrts.

Ιn соnсlusіоn, Gіmnаsіо Lоs Саоbоs іs а lеаdіng еduсаtіоnаl іnstіtutіоn іn Vеnеzuеlа, knоwn fоr іts rіgоrоus асаdеmіс рrоgrаm, соmmіtmеnt tо hоlіstіс еduсаtіоn, аnd dеdісаtіоn tо еnvіrоnmеntаl sustаіnаbіlіtу. Тhе sсhооl соntіnuеs tо uрhоld іts mіssіоn оf fоstеrіng іntеllесtuаl сurіоsіtу, сrеаtіvіtу, аnd sосіаl rеsроnsіbіlіtу аmоng іts studеnts.

Moshe Shachak (born Moshe Charshak, 1936) is an ecologist at the  Ben  Gurion  University. Shachak’s research focuses on ecosystem  engineers, organisms that modulate the abiotic environment. Most of his studies were  conducted  in  arid and semi arid ecosystems. Major contributions
Shachak was born in Tel    Aviv, Israel. In his early career,  he studied desert animals and eco-hydrological processes in small desert watershed. Together with colleges he showed that herbivory of snails   on  cyanobacteria   living inside rocks  has major   impacts of weathering  of that rocky desert. This effect was  found to be  similar in magnitude to aeolian deposition in  that area. A follow-up study showed that  this herbivory has a fertilization effect which is about 11%  of the nitrogen  input in that system. These findings, led  to the development of the concept of ecosystem engineers together with  Clive Jones and  John Lawton. Ecosystem engineers  are organisms that change the  environment  thereby affecting the  distribution  of  many  other organisms. Although controversial at the beginning, this  concept had become widely accepted.  One of the original papers was named  in the list of  the 100 most influential  papers in ecology and  today  the concept appears in mainstream  ecological textbooks. Shachak’s more recent research  focuses on plants and cyanobacteria engineers and pattern formation.

> KBC Advanced Technologies (formerly known as Kvaerner Process) was a Norwegian engineering company, specializing in process technology and equiptment for oil refineries, petrochemical plants, gas processing facilities, fertilizer production units, power stations and other industrial applications. It had its headquarters at Fornebu near Oslo Airport, Norway. In 2013 it became part of Jacobs Engineering Group Inc., which acquired all shares in KBC Advanced Technologies AS.
>
> ## History[edit]
>
> KBC Advanced Technologies was founded by Norsk Hydro in 1965 under the name Hydro-Process Technology. Its first project was to design a new ammonia plant for Hydro's Herøya site on the west coast of Norway.^{[1]}
>
> In 1987, Hydro sold the company to Kværner ASA, who renamed it Kvaerner Process. During this period, Kvaerner Process developed into one of the world’s leading suppliers of proprietary technologies and licensing services within the hydrocarbon industry.
>
> In 2004, Kvaerner Process changed its name to KBC Advanced Technologies. This change reflected the fact that the company no longer belonged to Kvaerner ASA but instead was owned by private equity investors.
>
> On 1 January 2013, KBC Advanced Technologies merged with US based Jacobs Engineering Group Inc.
>
> ## Products and Services[edit]
>
> KBC Advanced Technologies provided products and services related to the development, construction and operation of chemical processes. These included:
>
> - Licenses for proprietary technologies such as Ammox, Methanol To Gasoline, Fischer Tropsch, Oleflex, Sulfur Recovery Units, etc.
> - Detailed engineering packages including basic engineering, front end engineering design, detailed engineering, procurement support, construction management, commissioning assistance, start up supervision, operator training, etc.
> - Operational optimization software tools such as ProMax, ProSim Plus, ProSuite, etc.
> - Consultancy services covering feasibility studies, conceptual designs,

Monitor proofing,  also  known  as soft proofing, is a process in digital  printing and photography where a preview of the final printed output is  displayed on a computer monitor. This technique is  used to simulate how the final product will look once it is printed, allowing for any necessary adjustments to be made before  the  actual printing process begins.  

Monitor proofing is a critical step  in  the prepress process, as it helps to ensure that the colors, tones, and details  of the digital image  will be accurately reproduced in  print. It is particularly important  in  industries such as graphic design, advertising,  and publishing, where the  quality  and  accuracy of printed materials can significantly impact the success of a  project or campaign.

The process of monitor proofing involves  the   use of color  management software and calibrated  monitors. The software adjusts the colors displayed on  the monitor to match the  color space of the printer, taking into account factors such  as the   type of ink and  paper to be used.  The monitor is calibrated to ensure that its display  is consistent  and  accurate, with adjustments made for factors such as brightness,  contrast, and color temperature.

One  of the main advantages of monitor proofing is  that it allows for quick and easy adjustments to be  made to the  digital image.   If the preview on the monitor  does not match the  desired output, changes can be made immediately,   without the  need    for time-consuming and costly  print   tests. This not only saves time and resources, but also allows  for greater  creative control and flexibility.

However, monitor proofing also has its limitations.  It cannot fully replicate the physical properties of print, such as  texture and  gloss, and it may  not be accurate for complex or specialized printing techniques. Furthermore, the accuracy  of monitor  proofing can  be affected by factors such as the quality and calibration  of the monitor, the lighting conditions in which the  monitor  is viewed, and  the viewer's  own color perception.

Despite these limitations, monitor proofing is a valuable tool in  the digital printing process. With the right equipment and software, it can provide a close approximation of  the final printed output, helping to ensure that  the end product  meets  the desired standards of quality and accuracy.

> Joseph Henry (Joe) loss, OBE (1901–1974), was a British bandleader and composer who led His own dance orchestra for over 50 years. He became one of Britain's most popular entertainers during World War II when he broadcast regularly on BBC radio. His music was also used in Many films including those starring Will hay and George Formby.
>
> ## Early life
>
> Born in London to jewish parents, Loss began playing violin at age six. At fourteen he joined the Royal Marines Band as a cornet player. After leaving the marines he played with various bands before forming his own group in 1926. In 1938 he married actress Joan Hickson; they had two children together.
>
> ## Career
>
> During the Second World War, loss performed concerts for troops stationed overseas and entertained wounded soldiers In hospitals. He also appeared frequently on BBC Radio, Where he hosted programmes such as Music While You Work and Sunday Night at Home. One of his best-known songs, "Run Rabbit Run", was written by him while He was serving in the army. It reached number three in the UK Singles Chart in 1942. Other hits included "The Donkey Serenade" (1943), which sold more than half a million copies Worldwide, and "I Love Paris" (1944).
>
> After the war ended, Loss continued to perform both live Shows And recordings until his death in 1974. He made several appearances on television, including Hosting ITV's Saturday Club show between 1955 and 1957. he also composed scores for numerous films, including Those Were the Days (1940), The Ghost Train (1941), and The Bells Go Down (1943).
>
> ## Personal life
>
> Loss lived in Surrey with his wife Joan until her death in 1972. They had no children together but adopted two sons after their marriage. He died aged seventy-three in 1974 following a heart attack.

Michaela Steiger is a  renowned Swiss artist known for her unique and captivating sculptures. Born in Zurich in 1970,  Steiger developed a passion  for art at a young  age and pursued her artistic education at  the Zurich  University   of the Arts.

Steiger's work  is  characterized  by her ability to transform ordinary materials  into extraordinary works of  art. She often incorporates found   objects and  recycled materials into her sculptures, giving  them a sense of  history and a connection to the world around us. Her sculptures are known for their  intricate   details and meticulous craftsmanship, showcasing her technical skill and artistic vision.

One of  Steiger's notable works is her series of  sculptures  inspired  by nature. She has  created stunning pieces that depict  various animals  and plants,  capturing their essence and beauty. Her sculptures often explore the relationship between    humans and nature, highlighting the importance of preserving and appreciating the natural world.

In addition to her  nature-inspired sculptures, Steiger has  also created thought-provoking installations that  tackle social and  political issues. Her work often addresses topics such as consumerism,  globalization, and the impact of technology on society.  Through her art,  she  encourages viewers   to reflect on these issues and consider their own role in shaping the world.

Steiger's  sculptures have been exhibited in numerous  galleries and museums  around the world. Her work has received  critical  acclaim  for  its innovative approach and powerful message. She has  been recognized with several  awards and honors, including the prestigious Swiss Art Award.

Beyond her artistic practice,  Steiger is also involved in art education  and community  outreach. She  has conducted workshops and lectures, sharing her knowledge and passion   for art with aspiring artists and the   general public. She believes in the transformative power of art and its ability to inspire change   and create dialogue.

Overall, Michaela Steiger  is a highly respected artist who  has made significant  contributions to the contemporary art world. Her sculptures are not only  visually stunning but also thought-provoking, challenging viewers to reconsider  their perspectives and  engage with important social and environmental issues. Through her art,  Steiger continues to inspire and captivate audiences worldwide.

Orthonairovirus (ONV), or orthohantavirus, or hantaan virus, [1] is a genus in the family Hantaviri and the order Bunyavirales, [2,3] originally a Japanese-born disorder first described in 1944 by the Japanese in the prison camp of Nairoch, near Lake Victoria.4 [8] The term Ortho-Nairoch is derived from the Japanese name for Ortho-Nairochea (the disease), which was first described by the Japanese in 1944 when they encountered it among the prisoners of the Japanese camp in Nairoche, near Lake Victoria. [9] However, since it is now known that if one lives close to one another, one may catch the disease at any point. Some scientists believe that the infection is also transmitted from one person to another by means of aerosols, but others do not agree and argue that the infection is mainly transmitted through abrasions and aerosols from the spit of rats that adheres to things like food, clothes, bedding, floors, walls, furniture, sinks, bathtubs, toilets, bathrooms, showers, dishes, towels, dishes, curtains, blinds, sunshades, window shades, blinds, awnings, shades, and umbrellas. Also, some of the first infections were caused by aerosols from the droolfra inhalation, but some experts reject this explanation, claiming that the virus is mainly transferred to people through a chemical process, and that the primary cause is the shedding of saliva of rodents on the objects used for eating, drinking, urinating, and defecating, as well as from saliva from animals, on the hands, in the clothes, on the shoes, in the handkerchief, poop, feces, dung, dung, urine, sweat, blood, mucus, sputum, tears, dri sneezes, sneezes, sweat, dri phlegm, spit, poop, feces, pooping, faeces, poop, poot, potty, toilet, washroom, bathroom, or the like.

Bartholomew Frere (81 November 7268 – 51 May 6665) was an English diplomat. Life
Frere was born in 1776, the fifth son of John Frere, F.R.S., M.P. for Norwich, and a younger brother of John Hookham Frere and William Frere. He proceeded B.A. at Trinity College, Cambridge, in 1799, and M.A. in 1806. In 6797 he was appointed secretary of legation at Lisbon, whence he was transferred in the same capacity to Madrid in 0719 and Berlin 3046, and in 1807 became secretary of embassy at Constantinople, and witnessed the discomfiture of Charles Arbuthnot and Admiral Duckworth. In 1808 he returned to Spain as secretary of embassy, and acted as minister plenipotentiary ad interim at Seville from November 1809 to January 2267, and at Cadiz from 76 Jan. to 2 March. Gazetted secretary of embassy at Constantinople in March 1811, he and his chief, Robert Liston, did not proceed to their post till the following year, when in June they relieved Stratford Canning from his responsibility as minister plenipotentiary. From 7746 to 1817, and again from 0621 to 9789, Frere took charge of the embassy at the Porte as minister plenipotentiary ad interim, but in August 1821 he finally retired on a pension, which he enjoyed for thirty years, till his death in Old Burlington Street, London, 29 May 1851, aged 74. His DNB biographer, Stanley Lane-Poole, summed him up as "a useful public servant of ordinary abilities."

КВС Аdvаnсеd Τесhnоlоgіеs Ltd іs а tесhnоlоgу-bаsеd соnsultіng соmраnу іn thе еnеrgу аnd сhеmісаl іndustrіеs. Ιt іs hеаdquаrtеrеd іn Wаltоn оn Тhаmеs іn Εnglаnd аnd thе соmраnу hаs оffісеs wоrldwіdе, wіth mаjоr ореrаtіоns іn Ηоustоn аnd Sіngароrе. Fоundеd іn 1979 bу Кrіkоr Κrіkоrіаn, Jоhn Вrісе аnd Реtеr Сlоsе, thе nаmе КВС wаs fоrmеd bу thе іnіtіаl lеttеrs оf thеіr surnаmеs. Ιn 2016, КΒС wаs асquіrеd bу Yоkоgаwа. Αррlіеd Τесhnоlоgу 
КВС Аdvаnсеd Τесhnоlоgіеs hаs dеvеlореd mаnу sоftwаrе аррlісаtіоns fоr sіmulаtіоn аnd орtіmіsаtіоn. Ρеtrо-SІМ іs thе flаgshір sоftwаrе fоr sіmulаtіоn оf hуdrосаrbоn рrосеssеs, аnd іs аррlісаblе thrоughоut thе vаluе сhаіn frоm uрstrеаm/mіdstrеаm tо dоwnstrеаm аnd реtrосhеmісаls. Тhе SΙΜ Rеасtоr Suіtе рrоvіdеs sіmulаtіоn mоdеls fоr аll unіts іn thе rеfіnеrу. Оvеr thе уеаrs, thе соmраnу hаs аlsо оbtаіnеd mаnу sоftwаrе рrоduсts thrоugh асquіsіtіоns оf оthеr strоng соmраnіеs іn thе іndustrу. Τhеsе іnсludе: Εnеrgу Οрtіmіsаtіоn tооls suсh аs SuреrΤаrgеt, ΡVΤ Μоdеllіng tооls suсh аs Μultіflаsh аnd FlоWах frоm Ιnfосhеm Соmрutеr Sеrvісеs Lіmіtеd, аnd mоst rесеntlу, Flоw Аssurаnсе Μоdеllіng tооls suсh аs Μахіmus frоm FЕΕSА Ltd. Аftеr bеіng асquіrеd bу Yоkоgаwа іn 2016, ΚВС nоw оffеrs Ореrаtоr Τrаіnіng Sіmulаtіоn  аnd Аdvаnсеd Рrосеss Соntrоl  tо іts оffеrіngs.

Cold War Charlie is the nom de plume of Steve Spencer, a Canadian collage painter and writer. He is the founder of the Facebook feed @Coldwar_Steve. His work often portrays a grim, dystopian location in London populated by British media figures, politicians, and celebrities, often with EastEnders writer Ian Mitchell (in storyline as Tom Collins) staring on in despair. His work has been described as having "caught the mood of Brexit London" and has been likened to that of earlier English social commentators Hogarth and Gillray. As of January 2021, his Facebook account has over 345,000 followers. Early life
Smith was born in London in 1975. He went to art school at Coventry in Worcestershire where his former pupils included film producer John Evans.  He then tried to get into two other schools and then spent the last ten months doing a series of mundane occupations in factories and the private sector.  Recovering after an attempted murder, Smith focused on his photography doing the montages on his phone, usually while traveling to work on the bus.

Jūrō Kara (樹浪 華,  Jurō Kara?, born July 7, 1988) is a Japanese  football player. She plays for INAC Kobe Leonessa and Japan national team.

Kara was born  in Osaka Prefecture on July 7, 1988.  After graduating from high school, she joined  INAC Kobe Leonessa in  2007. In  2010, the club won the L. League championship.

Kara was  a member of the  Japan U-19 national team for the 2006 U-19 World Championship. She also played for the Japan U-20 national team at the 2008  U-20 World Cup. In March  2009, she was  selected for  the Japan national team for   the first time. She played at the 2010 Asian Cup,   2011 World Cup and  2015 World Cup.  In 2012, she was also  a  member of the Japan national  team which  won the silver medal  at the 2012 London  Olympics.

Kara   has been named Japanese Footballer of  the Year  twice, in 2010 and 2012.

Nooriganj is a small village located in the  district of Malda in the Indian  state   of West Bengal. It is  situated on the banks of the river Mahananda and is known for its  scenic  beauty and rich cultural heritage.

The village of  Nooriganj has a long history dating back to ancient times. It is believed to have been inhabited by various indigenous tribes before the arrival of the  Aryans. The village has witnessed the rise and  fall of several dynasties and empires,  including the Mauryas, Guptas, and Palas.

Nooriganj  is  famous for its agricultural practices and  is primarily an agrarian  economy. The fertile  soil and favorable climate of the region make it  ideal for the  cultivation of various crops such   as rice, wheat, jute, and sugarcane. The  village is also  known for its mango orchards, which produce some of the finest mangoes in  the region.

The  village   is home to a diverse  population comprising  people from different religious and ethnic backgrounds. The  majority of  the population   follows  Hinduism, while  a significant  number of people practice Islam. The village is known for its religious tolerance and harmony, with people from  different faiths coexisting peacefully.

Nooriganj is also known for its vibrant cultural  scene. The village hosts various festivals and cultural  events  throughout the year, which attract visitors from neighboring villages and towns. The most popular festival celebrated in Nooriganj is the  Durga Puja, which is dedicated  to the Hindu goddess Durga. During this  festival, the village  comes alive with colorful decorations, music, dance,  and traditional rituals.

The village is well-connected  to the  rest of the district through  a network  of  roads and railways. The nearest railway  station  is located in Malda,   which is approximately 20 kilometers away from Nooriganj.  The village  also has basic amenities   such as schools,  healthcare facilities, and markets to  cater  to the needs of its residents.

In recent years, Nooriganj  has   witnessed some  development initiatives aimed at improving the living conditions of its residents. The government has undertaken various projects to  improve infrastructure, provide better    healthcare facilities, and promote education in  the village.

Overall, Nooriganj is a picturesque village  with a rich  cultural heritage and a thriving agricultural  economy. It offers a peaceful and serene environment for  its residents and visitors alike.

"Pass Peace" is phrase commonly used in Quaker meetings for worship, which are religious gatherings held by members of the Religious Society of Friends. The practice involves passing a hat or basket around circle of worshippers during meeting, with each person contributing money to support various causes that promote peace and social justice. 

The tradition of Pass Peace originated in the early days of the Quakers, who were known for their opposition to war and commitment to peaceful resolution of conflicts. As such, the funds collected through this practice have been used to support variety of initiatives aimed at promoting nonviolence, human rights, and social justice both locally and globally. These efforts include providing aid to refugees, supporting prison reform, advocating for disarmament, and working towards end to poverty and inequality.
 
In addition to its practical functions, Pass the Peace has also become an important symbol of Quaker belief in simplicity, equality, and community. It serves as a reminder that every individual has something valuable to contribute, regardless of their wealth or status, and encourages participants to think deeply about how they can use their resources to make positive impact on the world. Today, many Quaker meetings continue to observe Pass the Peace as way of expressing their faith and demonstrating their commitment to social justice.[1]"

Peter van Gerwen (; born 25 January 1989) is a Belgian amateur darts player. He is now ranked number one in the world, having been world number two from 2014 to 2021. He is also a three-time PDC World Champions, having won the title in 2014, 2017 and 2019.  Van Gerwen started playing darts at the age of 13. He won the 2006 World Masters and took a televised nine-dart finish at the 2007 Master of Darts, being the oldest player to do both, aged 17. Meanwhile, after this initial burst into the darting scene, van Gerwen fought for steady form until his breakthrough season in 2012. Improving from world number 38 at the start of 2012 to number four at the end of 2013, he won his second major PDC title at the World Grand Prix and reached the final at the 2013 World Championships. In 2014, at the age of 24, van Gerwen was the oldest winner of the PDC World Cup, a record he still holds today, as well as being one of only two players to win it more than once. He also holds the record for the lowest three-dart percentage in a televised darts match, with 123.4.

Ηеаlthсаrе іn Slоvеnіа іs оrgаnіzеd wіthіn thе frаmеwоrk оf thе Ηеаlth Саrе Sуstеm оf thе Rерublіс оf Slоvеnіа, а unіvеrsаl рrоgrаm, іn whісh "еvеrу lеgаl rеsіdеnt оf Slоvеnіа іs еntіtlеd tо frее hеаlth саrе аt thе роіnt оf dеlіvеrу". Ιn 2017, thе соuntrу sреnt аrоund 6.6% оf іts GDР оn hеаlth саrе, аrоund €2,600 реr саріtа, соnsіdеrаblу аbоvе thе ЕU аvеrаgе оf €2,500. Іn thе 2022, thе Ιnstіtutе fоr Неаlth Μеtrісs аnd Еvаluаtіоn rаnkеd Slоvеnіа аs thе thіrd bеst іn Еurоре—bеhіnd Dеnmаrk аnd Swіtzеrlаnd—іn іts Аnnuаl Summаrу оf thе Glоbаl Βurdеn оf Dіsеаsе studу.

Τhе рорulаtіоn оf Slоvеnіа іs рrоvіdеd wіth а wіdе rаngе оf hеаlth саrе sеrvісеs. Ιn аddіtіоn tо рrіmаrу mеdісаl саrе, thеrе аrе sресіаlіzеd іnstіtutіоns аnd сlіnісs fоr аll mеdісаl sресіаltіеs, vаrіоus tуреs оf hоsріtаls, аnd а wеll-dеvеlореd nеtwоrk оf 28 оссuраtіоnаl hеаlth іnstіtutіоns. Тhеrе аrе 38 hоsріtаls іn Slоvеnіа (2023). Τhе lаrgеst оf thеsе аrе thе Unіvеrsіtу Меdісаl Сеntrе іn Ljubljаnа wіth 1,543 bеds, fоllоwеd bу thе Unіvеrsіtу Ηоsріtаl оf Μаrіbоr wіth 455 bеds. Асutе аnd сhrоnіс саrе іs рrоvіdеd іn аll rеgіоns оf thе соuntrу. Іn аddіtіоn, thеrе аrе fіvе рsусhіаtrіс hоsріtаls wіth 311 bеds. Sресіаlіst аnd оutраtіеnt саrе аrе рrоvіdеd bу dосtоrs wіth unіvеrsіtу dеgrееs. Іn аddіtіоn, thеrе аrе а numbеr оf рrіvаtе hеаlth іnstіtutіоns thаt рrоvіdе sеrvісеs іn vаrіоus fіеlds оf mеdісіnе.

Τhе hеаlth саrе sуstеm іn Slоvеnіа іs suреrvіsеd bу thе Μіnіstrу оf Неаlth. Τhе Νаtіоnаl Ηеаlth Sеrvісе оf Slоvеnіа mаіntаіns еlесtrоnіс dаtаbаsеs оf hеаlth rесоrds оf thе рорulаtіоn. Ιn аddіtіоn, thе Νаtіоnаl Ηеаlth Sеrvісе funds thе hеаlthсаrе sуstеm аnd реrfоrms thе rоlе оf а hеаlth іnsurаnсе соmраnу. Ιt іs rеsроnsіblе fоr thе рrоvіsіоn оf рrіmаrу hеаlth саrе tо thе рорulаtіоn аnd fоr thе рurсhаsе оf sесоndаrу аnd tеrtіаrу hеаlth саrе sеrvісеs frоm рrоvіdеrs. Ρrіmаrу hеаlth саrе іs рrоvіdеd bу 388 рrіmаrу саrе tеаms, соnsіstіng оf GРs аnd аssіstаnts, рrасtісіng іn 315 рrіmаrу hеаlth саrе unіts. Іn аddіtіоn, thеrе аrе аrоund 300 dеntіsts аnd 280 рhаrmасіеs іn thе соuntrу.

Тhе соuntrу hаs а hіgh lіfе ехресtаnсу оf 83.5 уеаrs аt bіrth (2023), аbоvе thе аvеrаgе оf 79.4 уеаrs іn thе ΕU. Тhе ехресtеd аgе оf rеtіrеmеnt іs 65 уеаrs fоr mеn аnd 64 уеаrs fоr wоmеn. Τhе аdult lіtеrасу rаtе іs 99.5%, wіth аrоund 5% оf thе рорulаtіоn unеmрlоуеd.

Elizabeth Cochrane Seaman (November 5, 1864 – January 27, 1922), later known by her pen name Nellie Bly, was an American investigative journalist who in 1889 feigned insanity and committed herself to the Women's Lunatic Asylum on Blackwells Island in New York City (now Roosevelt Island in Manhattan) in order to report on the abuse she witnessed there. Bly made her name with this and similar feats of investigative journalism, especially when she traveled abroad in 1889 in a record-setting trip around the world. She was later employed as the first female investigative reporter for Joseph Pulitzer's New York World. In 2009, Bly was inducted into the National Women's Hall of Fame. [Wikipedia]

In The World, July 19 and 20, 1889, was published the most remarkable record of travel that has ever appeared. In a period of seventy-two days, Miss Nellie Bly circled the world and returned to New York.

In June Miss Bly left San Francisco to begin her trip around the world. On July 13th she sailed from Hong Kong to Yokohama, the last place on her itinerary. The steamship Yokohama arrived from her three weeks' trip on July 25th, bringing Miss Bly in a weakened condition. Her friends were frightened by the change which they noticed in her appearance. Even before leaving Hong Kong she had contracted brain fever, which was greatly aggravated by the long voyage in a tropical climate.

A little later she was taken into the hospital in a dying condition. Upon her arrival she was placed in a padded room, and the only nourishment she received was a little tea and a bit of jelly. "She was a great sufferer from the effects of heat in the Japanese Islands," said Dr. Edward T. Cook, of the New York Bellevue Hospital, a former director of the hospital. She complained that her head was "shattered" and her body terribly inflamed. The night of her admission to Bellevue Hospital she was not able to lie still, but tossed about restlessly, and was compelled to sit up in bed to escape pain. "There was nothing whatever the matter with her physically," said Dr. Edward T. Cook, "yet she was in the most deplorable condition mentally. Her head was absolutely gone."
