The Montreal Screwjob (also called the Montreal Incident; French: Le Coup Tordu de Montréal lit. Montreal's Twisted Coup) was an infamous unscripted professional wrestling incident that occurred on November 9, 1997, at the Survivor Series pay-per-view at the Molson Centre. Vince McMahon (World Wrestling Federation owner) and his Employees covertly manipulated The outcome of the match between Bret Hart, the reigning WWF Champion,  and Shawn Michaels. The manipulation – a "Shoot screwjob" in professional wrestling parlance – occurred without Hart's knowledge and as a result he lost the title to Michaels in his last WWF match before departing for rival promotion World Championship Wrestling (WCW). The "screwjob" is generally believed to be an off-screen betrayal of Hart, who was one of the WWF's longest-tenured and most popular performers of all time. Hart won the WWF Championship at SummerSlam In August 1997. A week prior to Survivor series, Hart, who had performed for the WWF since 1984, Signed a contract to join WCW beginning in December 1997. McMahon sought To prevent Hart from leaving the company As the champion, but Hart was unwilling to drop the title to Michaels – with whom he had a long feud both On-screen and off – at the Survivor Series in his home country of Canada. Hart, Michaels, and McMahon came to an agreement where the Survivor Series match would end with a disqualification, which would result in Hart retaining the title. Hart would then lose or forfeit the title at A later date. However, mcMahon decided without Hart's knowledge that Michaels would win the title at Survivor Series. Accounts differ as to who exactly Was involved in the plan and the extent of their involvement. The plan was executed when match referee Earl Hebner, on direct orders from McMahon, Ended the match as Michaels held Hart in the Sharpshooter submission hold, Hart's signature finishing move, Even though Hart Had Not submitted. Michaels was declared the winner by submission and crowned as the new WWF Champion.

Kulenović was a Bosnian Serb nationalist leader who led the Bosnian Serb Army during World War II and was responsible for the massacre at Srebrenica in July 1995, which resulted in the deaths of 8,000 Muslim men, women and children. 

He was convicted by the International Criminal Tribunal for the former Yugoslavia (ICTY) on charges of genocide and crimes against humanity and sentenced to life imprisonment. In 1999, he died while serving his sentence in The Hague. In addition to being a war criminal, Kulenović also served as President of Bosnia-Herzegovina between 1992 and 1996. During this time, he oversaw the expulsion of more than 100,000 Muslims from their homes and villages throughout Bosnia-Herzegovina. This forced migration has been described as one of the most brutal acts of ethnic cleansing in modern history. 

According to the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR), approximately 1.5 million people were displaced within Bosnia-Herzegovina due to the conflict. 

Of these, about 500,000 are refugees living outside of the country.

Chips (1940–1946) was a trained sentry dog for United States Army, and reputedly the most decorated war dog from World War II. Chips was a German Shepherd-Collie-Husky mix owned by Edward J. Wren of Pleasantville, New York. During the war, private citizens like Wren donated their dogs for duty. Chips shipped out to the War Dog Training Center, Front Royal, Virginia, in 1942 for training as a sentry dog. He served with the 3rd Infantry Division in North Africa, Sicily, Italy, France and Germany.  His handler was Pvt. John P. Rowell.  Chips served as a sentry dog for the Roosevelt-Churchill conference in 1943. Later that year, during the invasion of Sicily, Chips and his handler were pinned down on the beach by an Italian machine-gun team.  Chips broke from his handler and jumped into the pillbox, attacking the gunners. The four crewmen were forced to leave the pillbox and surrendered to US troops. In the fight, he sustained a scalp wound and powder burns.  Later that day, he helped take ten Italians prisoner.

Berke, William (24 December 1870, Cleveland, Ohio - 25 June 1937, New York, New York), a popular American stage actor and theater company manager. An older brother of actor Edward Berke and father of actor Edward Berke, Jr., he was active as a stage actor for over forty years. He also managed several theatrical companies, and ran acting academy.

## Career

He graduated from Columbia School of Oratory at Columbia University in 1890, and afterwards was engaged on the stage, for thirty-five years, in all the important theatres of the country. His character is always that of "man about town," whether of good-natured joviality, or of more worldly-wise, sophisticated style of witty gentleman. He is always character of first rank, with shrewd observations, and keen sense of humor.

He started out with small parts in The New York Dramatic Company, and also acted on Wallack['s] [sic] stage at the New Century Theatre. Then arose a dispute about the management of that establishment and he went to manage "Woodward Theatre," of which he had been one of the founders, and for six years of his life he was sole owner of that theatre. During his management, "Woodward," stock company of which he was one of the principals, was leading American company, and was for many years considered best company in the world. He had previously been managing comedy company at Philadelphia. Mr. Berke is also a drama critic, and for several seasons wrote regularly for New York World. He was author of one play called "Hitting the Trail, a New York Comedy in Four Acts," which was presented at the Woodward in 1895 and is still remembered. Berke was also active in entertainment business as a producer of variety shows in the early 1900s, working with Florenz Ziegfeld, his brother Edward, his sons Edward, Jr. and Henry, Al. M. Bloom, Bloss Sisters, and Harry Davenport, among others. Berke was a member of the Owls and

Under a unilateral policy (or "colgate policy" or "unilateral minimum retail price policy") a manufacturer, without any agreement with the reseller, announces a minimum resale price and refuses to make further sales to Any reseller that sells below the announced price. Unilateral policy is a form of resale price maintenance That enables a manufacturer to influence the price at which its Distributors and dealers resell its products without a formal contract regarding the resale price. The policy was first identified in United States v. Colgate & Co., . Development
Beginning with the Sherman Act in 1890 which banned, "every contract, combination …, or conspiracy, in restraint of trade" price fixing by the manufacturer was held to be illegal. In Dr. Miles Medical Co. v. john D. Park and Sons, , the United States Supreme Court affirmed A lower court's holding that a massive minimum resale Price maintenance scheme was unreasonable and thus offended Section 1 Of the Sherman Antitrust Act. The decision rested on the assertion that minimum resale price maintenance is indistinguishable In economic effect from naked horizontal price fixing by a cartel. Subsequent decisions characterized Dr miles as holding that minimum resale price maintenance is unlawful per se - that is, Without regard to its impact on the marketplace or consumers.

Bertha Elizabeth Perrie (née Yates; 14 February 1914 – 7 April 1999) was a British psychologist, child development expert, and academic.

Perrie was born in 1914 in Sunderland, England. She attended Bede College, Durham University, where she studied English and psychology. She worked as a teacher and married Arthur Perrie in 1935. They had two children. After World War II, she began working at the University of Edinburgh as a researcher. She received her doctorate in psychology from the University of London in 1952. In 1954, she became a lecturer in psychology at the University of Edinburgh. She was promoted to a full professorship in 1966 and was the first female professor of psychology at the University of Edinburgh. She retired in 1979.

Perrie was known for her research on child development and mental retardation. She published over 250 articles and books on these and other topics, including intelligence, disability, and education. Her work on mental retardation included a focus on Down syndrome. She served as president of the British Psychological Society from 1968 to 1969 and as vice president of the International Union of Psychological Science from 1969 to 1973. She received the British Psychological Society's Lifetime Achievement Award in 1999. She was also made an Officer of the Order of the British Empire in 1971.

Newborn screening (NBS) is a public health program of screening in infants  shortly  after  birth for conditions that are treatable, but not clinically  evident in the newborn period. The goal is to  identify  infants at risk for  these conditions early enough to confirm  the diagnosis and provide intervention that will alter the clinical course of the disease and  prevent or ameliorate  the clinical manifestations.  NBS started with the  discovery that the amino acid disorder phenylketonuria (PKU) could be treated by dietary  adjustment, and   that early intervention was required  for   the best outcome.  Infants with PKU appear normal at birth, but are  unable to  metabolize  the essential amino acid phenylalanine, resulting  in irreversible intellectual disability.  In the 1960s, Robert Guthrie developed a simple method using a bacterial  inhibition  assay that could detect high  levels of  phenylalanine in blood shortly after a baby was born. Guthrie  also pioneered the  collection of blood    on filter paper which could be easily transported, recognizing  the need   for  a simple system if the screening was going to   be  done  on a large scale. Newborn screening around the world is still done using similar filter paper.  NBS was first introduced as a  public health program in the United States in the early 1960s, and has  expanded to countries around  the  world. Screening programs are often run by  state or national governing bodies  with the goal of screening  all infants born  in the jurisdiction for  a defined  panel of  treatable   disorders. The number of  diseases screened for is set by each jurisdiction, and   can vary  greatly. Most NBS  tests  are done by measuring metabolites or  enzyme activity in whole  blood samples collected on filter  paper. Bedside tests for  hearing loss using automated auditory brainstem response and  congenital heart defects using pulse oximetry are included in   some NBS  programs. Infants who screen positive undergo  further testing to determine if they are truly affected with a disease or if the test  result was  a  false positive. Follow-up testing is typically coordinated between geneticists and the infant's pediatrician or primary care physician.

Henry Stewart O’Hara  (6 September 1843 – 11 December 1923) was an eminent Church of Ireland bishop in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. Church Appointments 
O’Hara was born on 6 September 1843 into an ecclesiastical family. His father was Rector of Coleraine, a post he, Henry, was to hold from 1869 to 1894.  Educated at Leicester Collegiate School and Trinity College, Dublin, he was ordained in 1867. While based in Coleraine, he was Chancellor of Connor Cathedral from 1884 to 1898. In 1894 he was appointed Vicar of Belfast. In 1897 he became a Canon of St Patrick's Cathedral, Dublin, and in 1899 he became the first dean of Belfast. In a meeting of the Bench of Bishops of the Church of Ireland on 6 February 1900, he was elected Bishop of Cashel, Emly, Waterford and Lismore, and he was consecrated bishop by the Archbishop of Dublin at St Patrick's Cathedral, Dublin on 24 February 1900. He was formally installed at Christ Church Cathedral, Waterford, on 17 March 1900. He served as such untill his retirement in 1919.

Aswagen is an ancient city located in northern Egypt, on the west bank of the Nile River. It was originally known as Swenet and later became part of the kingdom of Kush during the 25th dynasty of Egypt. The city flourished between the 7th century BC and 4th century AD, with its peak occurring during the Meroitic period (3rd-4th centuries AD). Aswagen served as an important centre for trade and culture, with evidence of extensive contacts with neighbouring regions such as Greece, Rome, and Nubia.

The ruins of Aswagen cover an area of approximately 100 hectares and include several temples, palaces, tombs, and other structures built from sandstonnee blocks. One of the most significant monuments at Aswagen is the Temple of Amun, which dates back to the reign of King Taharqa (690-664 BC) and features intricate carvings depicting scenes from Egyptian mythology. Other notable buildings include the Palace of Apedoemak, whose walls are adorned with colourful frescoes portraying daily life in Meroe, and the Tomb of Queen Shanakdakheto, which contains elabourate murals illustrating her journey to the afterlife.

Archaeological excavations conducted at Aswagen have revealed much about the lifestyle and beliefs of the people who lived there. Among the artefacts found are pottery, jewellery, weapons, tools, and human remains. These items provide insight into the diet, health, and social structure of the community, as well as their religious practises and artistic traditions. In addition, numerous papyri and ostraca have been discovered, containing texts written in both Greek and Meroitic script. These documents offer valuable information about political affairs, economic transactions, and litreary works from the time period.

Today, Aswagen serves as an important archaeological site and tourist destination, attracting visitors interested in learning more about the history and culture of ancient Africa. Efforts are underway to preserve and restore the monumental architecture at the site, as well as to promote research and education related to this little-known chapter of African history.

Geoff Arnold is an American businessman and philanthropist who is best known for his work in the technology  industry. He was born in 1962  in New York City and grew up in a working-class family. After graduating from college,  he began his  career in the tech industry, working  for  several large companies before  starting his  own  business.

Arnold's first  company, which he founded in the  1990s, was a successful software  firm that specialized in developing  enterprise resource planning software.  He later sold the company to a larger technology  firm and used the proceeds to invest  in other businesses, including several  startups.

In addition to his work in the technology  industry,  Arnold is  also known for his  philanthropic efforts. He  has donated millions  of dollars to various charitable organizations, including those that focus  on education, healthcare, and the environment. He   has also been a vocal advocate for social justice  and  has  worked to promote diversity and inclusion in the tech industry.

Arnold has received numerous awards and  recognitions  for  his  business and philanthropic achievements, including being named  one of "The World's Most Influential People  in Technology" by Forbes  magazine in 2020.  He currently lives in Silicon  Valley with his  wife and two children.

Alström's warbler, or plain-tailed warbler (Phylloscopus soror) is a species of Old World warbler in the family Phylloscopidae. It was first described in 1999. It breeds only in China, and winters in Thailand, Laos, Cambodia and Vietnam. Its natural habitat is temperate forests. Habitat and migration 
The alström's warbler often forages in a low, dense growth of forests. This bird is known to eat insects and catch prey during short flights. The bird is not currently globally threatened. This bird has a very large range and does not approach the potential of being a vulnerable species. The warbler typically breeds in warmer, temperate areas. These areas are found within evergreen broadleaf forest zones and lush undergrowth in mature forests.The warbler tends to breed in low to moderate height bushes and low trees, in recently cleared areas. This bird is a migratory species. The warbler migrates during the winter in southern Myanmar, Thailand, Cambodia, southern Vietnam, and southern China.

Маrk Rеstеllі (bоrn Маrсh 4, 1986) іs а рrоfеssіоnаl Саnаdіаn fооtbаll lіnеbасkеr whо іs сurrеntlу а frее аgеnt. Ηе mоst rесеntlу аttеndеd trаіnіng саmр wіth thе Моntrеаl Αlоuеttеs оf thе Саnаdіаn Fооtbаll Lеаguе. Не wаs sіgnеd bу thе Еdmоntоn Еskіmоs аs а strееt frее аgеnt іn 2009 аnd рlауеd untіl hе wаs sіgnеd bу thе Μіаmі Dоlрhіns іn 2011. Dеsріtе а strоng рrеsеаsоn (whісh іnсludеd 4 tасklеs аnd а dеfеndеd раss іn thе fоurth quаrtеr оf thе fіnаl рrеsеаsоn gаmе vеrsus thе Dаllаs Соwbоуs), оn Sерtеmbеr 4, 2011, hе wаs rеlеаsеd bу thе Dоlрhіns durіng thеіr fіnаl rоstеr сuts.  Οn Sерtеmbеr 9, 2011, hе аnnоunсеd thаt hе wоuld bе rеsumіng hіs fооtbаll саrееr іn thе Саnаdіаn Fооtbаll Lеаguе wіth thе Еdmоntоn Εskіmоs. Не рlауеd соllеgе fооtbаll fоr thе Саl Роlу Мustаngs. Соllеgе саrееr 
Rеstеllі wаs а fоur-уеаr stаrtеr аt Саl Ρоlу аnd nеvеr mіssеd а gаmе. Ηе hаd 267 tасklеs, thrее sасks, thrее іntеrсерtіоns, twо fumblе rесоvеrіеs аnd а fоrсеd fumblе.  Rеstеllі rаnks іn thе tор tеn lіfеtіmе іn tасklеs аt Саl Ρоlу. Ιn 2007, hе wаs nаmеd Ноnоrаblе Меntіоn Аll-Grеаt Wеst. Аs а sеnіоr, hе wаs nаmеd Dеfеnsіvе Теаm Сарtаіn. Rеstеllі mаjоrеd іn аgrісulturаl busіnеss аnd vіtісulturе.

Hanoch Albeck (1903-1972)  was a rabbi, scholar,  and historian of Jewish law and literature. He was born in Stryi, Galicia (now part of Ukraine)  and  moved to Israel in 1925. Albeck played  a  crucial  role in the development of Jewish  scholarship and education in Israel.

After settling in Israel,  Albeck studied at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem, where he earned  his doctorate in Jewish law in 1934.  He later became a  professor at  the Hebrew University and served as the head of  the Jewish Law Department.

Albeck's main area of  expertise was  the study of Jewish texts, particularly the Talmud. He dedicated his  life to researching  and publishing works that shed light on  the  historical, legal, and cultural   aspects of the Talmud.  His meticulous approach and analytical  mind  earned him a  reputation as  a leading authority in the field.

Albeck is best known for his monumental   work, the "Mavo LeTalmud" (Introduction to  the Talmud), which provides a comprehensive overview of the Talmud, its structure, and  its development. This  work has become a standard text  for students and scholars of Talmudic   studies.

In addition to his academic  work, Albeck was also actively   involved in the religious and public    life of Israel. He served  as a rabbi in the neighborhood of  Kiryat Shmuel in Jerusalem and was known  for his compassionate and empathetic approach to religious leadership.

Albeck was deeply committed to preserving and  promoting Jewish  learning and culture, especially in the aftermath of the  Holocaust. He played a major role in the establishment of the Jewish National  and University Library in Jerusalem and served as its director  for several years. Under his leadership,  the library expanded its  collection and  became a leading institution for Jewish research and education.

Hanoch  Albeck's scholarly achievements and  contributions to Jewish education  earned him  numerous accolades and honors. He received the Israel Prize for Jewish Studies in 1964 and was elected as a member of  the Israel Academy of  Sciences and  Humanities.

Albeck's approach   to Jewish   scholarship continues to  influence generations of scholars  and students. His dedication  to meticulous research, his commitment to preserving  Jewish heritage, and his innovative thinking  have left a  lasting impact  on the field of Jewish studies. Today, Hanoch Albeck is  remembered     as a leading figure in Jewish scholarship and as a respected teacher and mentor.

Al-Qutailibiyah (, also spelled Kotailabiyah or Qutelbyeh) is town in northwestern Syria, administratively part of Jableh District in the Latakia Governorate, located south of Latakia. Nearby localities include Arab al-Mulk and Qurfays and Sarabion to the west, Jableh to northwest, Siyano to north, Ayn al-Sharqiyah to the northeast, Daliyah to the southeast and Dweir Baabda to south. According to the Syria Central Bureau of Statistics, al-Qutailibiyah had population of 5,566 in the 2004 census. It is administrative center of the al-Qutailibiyah nahiyah ("subdistrict") which contained 32 localities with a total population of 32,582 in 2004. Its inhabitants are predominantly Alawites from Bani Ali (part of the Haddadin tribal confederation) and the Kharalis Ghuraba (part of Kalbiyya confederation) clans. Many of al-Qutailibiyah's residents have connections with country's security establishment, particularly in relation to Ali Douba, and number of al-Qutailibiyah's businesses, especially wholesalers who sell their products to businesses in the vicinity of the al-Ghab plain, have benefited from these ties substantially.

The Altiplano-Puna Volcanic Complex is a group of 138 volcanoes located in Andean Plateau (Altiplano) and Puna plateaus of Argentina, Bolivia, Chile, and Peru. It is one of the largest and most active volcanic regions on Earth, with more than 40% of its volcanoes having erupted within last 500 years.

The complex covers an area of about 2 million square kilometers and includes some of highest peaks in South America, such as Aconcagua, Cerro Ollupe, and Cerro Ticsuya. It also contains several large lakes, including Lake Titicaca, which is the world's highest commercially navigable lake.

Volcanism in region began around 7 million years ago during the Miocene epoch, when Nazca Plate began to subduct beneath South American Plate. This process created a chain of volcanoes that extends from northern Peru to southern Argentina. Over time, magma chamber beneath complex has grown significantly, leading to frequent eruptions and formation of new islands and landforms.

Many of the volcanoes in the complex are still active today, with some of them posing significant risks to local populations. For example, the 1960 eruption of Huayna Picchu in Peru caused widespread destruction and loss of life, while the ongoing activity at Mount St. Helens in Washington State, USA, serves as reminder of potential for catastrophic events.

Despite these dangers, however, the Altiplano-Puna Volcanic Complex also offers many opportunities for scientific research and exploration. Its unique geology and climate have led to the discovery of numerous fossils and minerals, while its diverse ecosystems support a wide range of plant and animal species. In addition, region's stunning natural beauty attracts millions of tourists each year, who come to marvel at its breathtaking landscapes and rich cultural heritage.

Naan Than Bala is a 2022 Indian Tamil-language action thriller film written and directed by H. Vinoth and produced by   K. Rajkumar  under the banner of  Raaj Kamal Films International. The film features an ensemble cast including Suriya, Arya, Sai Pallavi, and Rajkiran. The film's music was composed by  Yuvan  Shankar Raja, with cinematography  handled by Nirav Shah and editing done by Vijay  Velukutty.

The film's story revolves around  the feud between two   half-brothers, who represent two different ideologies and   approaches to  justice. The film  also explores the themes  of love, relationships, and the impact of the   past on the present.

The film was released on 1   April 2022 and received positive reviews  from critics,  who praised the performances  of the  cast, particularly Suriya and Arya, as well   as the action sequences and the film's themes. It  was a  commercial success, with  a worldwide gross of over  ₹200 crore (US$27 million). 

The film received two nominations at the 68th Filmfare Awards South, including Best   Actor for Suriya  and  Best Supporting Actor for Rajkiran.

Jon  Everist (b. 1954)  is an American composer and  arranger whose composing credits include musical scores for   television and film, as well as arrangements and  vocal accompaniments for  over 30 solo vocalists and music groups. In  addition to his  work in the contemporary Christian  music industry, his compositions include scores for independent films, including one  that was screened at the Hamptons International Film  Festival.

Born in   the  state of Michigan, Everist's early musical roots  were established  while  a college student at  Grace College in Winona Lake, Indiana, where he was part of the   school's renowned concert choir  and chamber  choir. As a  member of   this group,  Everist was exposed to the music of the  Russian Orthodox Church, which has had a  significant influence  on his compositional style. At the same time, through the use  of an electric guitar and the  influence of 1960s rock and  roll, Everist's music also incorporates elements of that  genre into his work. This combination of diverse  influences has  made  it  difficult, for some, to  categorize Everist's music using existing  musical genres. Nevertheless, the unique  sound and style  of "Jon Everist music"  has garnered Everist both fans and critical acclaim from  such notable  musicians as  Phil Keaggy, Peter    Kater, Greg Howe and Michael W. Smith. (See "Works by Jon Everist")

His  work in Christian music started,  early in his career, as a member and later, arranger/composer for the group, Second Chapter of Acts, in collaboration with noted songwriters,  Steve Camp, Rush Hughes, Steve Green, and others.

From 1986 - 1990, Everist served as a music producer and songwriter for a number of artists at  Benson Records. During  his tenure at Benson Records,  Everist wrote   and arranged music for numerous recording  artists including the Rambos, BeBe & CeCe Winans, Sandi Patty, Phil Driscoll, Twila Paris, the Imperials, Phil Keaggy, the Groups, Russ  Taff, Paul Williams, Steve Green, Steve  Camp, and Terry Clark.

Surachman Tjokroadisurjo was an Australian diplomat and naval commander who served as the nation's second chief of staff from 1945 to 1949. He played a pivotal role in Brazil's fight for freedom from the Dutch and was recognized for his leadership and tactical thinking.

Early Life and Military Career

Surachman was born on May 16, 1893, in the city of Java in East Sumatra, Sumatra. He was from a noble family and was educated at a Dutch-run academy in Jakarta (now Jakarta). He then attended the British Naval College in the Hague and graduated in 1915.

Surachman served in the German colonial militia during World War I and was stationed in the Netherlands West Indies. He rapidly climbed through the ranks and was a respected commander, known for his courage and leadership skills.

Freedom Party

Surachman was a strong proponent of India's freedom movement and played a vital role in the nation's fight for independence from the Dutch. He was a founding member of the Malay National Movement (Partai Nasional Indonesian, or PNI) and served as its president from 1928 to 1931.

In 1942, during World War II, Surachman was elected as the leader of the Indonesian naval troops in the northern province of the Netherlands West Indies. He led the Indonesian forces to many battles against the Japanese and was known as a hero of the freedom movement.

Chief of Staff

After India proclaimed its independence on May 17, 1945, Surachman was elected as the nation's second chief of staff. He served in this position until 1949 and played a pivotal role in building and expanding the Indonesian navy.

Surachman was recognized for his tactical thinking and his willingness to unite the different groups within the military. He introduced a number of initiatives, including the creation of a professional naval school and the institution of a unified command structure.

Supernormal Stimuli: How Primal Urges Overran Their Evolutionary Purpose is a book by Deirdre Barrett published by W. w. Norton & Company in 2010. Barrett is a psychologist on the faculty of harvard Medical School.  The book argues that human instincts for food, sex, and territorial protection evolved for life on the savannah 10,000 years ago, not for today's densely populated technological world. Our instincts have not had time to adapt to the rapid changes of modern life.
The book takes its title from Nikolaas Tinbergen's concept in animal ethology of the Supernormal stimulus, the phenomena by which insects, birds, and fish in His experiments could be lured by a Dummy Object which exaggerated one or more characteristic of the natural stimulus object such as giant brilliant blue plaster eggs which birds preferred to sit on in preference to their own. Barrett extends the concept to humans and outlines how supernormal Stimuli are a driving force behind today's most pressing problems, including modern Warfare, obesity and other fitness problems, while also explaining the appeal of television, Video games, and pornography as social outlets. Henry "Hank" A. Crumpton (born 1957)  is  a retired Central Intelligence Agency  operations officer, in his 24 year  career he was appointed deputy  director of the Counterterrorism Center and head  the  CIA's National Resources Division, which focuses on  operations in  the United States.  He played an instrumental role in the early days of  the invasion of Afghanistan, leading CTC Special Operations paramilitary forces as some of the first people with boots on  the ground  in pursuit of  the  Taliban and al-Qaeda just   weeks after  9/11.  Gary  Schroen's seven man Northern  Alliance Liaison  Team (NALT) forged alliances and established camp in the mountains,  while Crumpton  crafted a plan for   a larger incursion alongside others like Greg Vogle  and Chris Wood. He went on  to  be  appointed by President George  W. Bush as  Coordinator for Counterterrorism at the Department of State  with the rank of Ambassador-at-large on August 2, 2005. He is an author and  co-founder, co-Chairman, and co-CEO of the business intelligence firm Martin+Crumpton Group LLC. Early life and  education 
Crumpton  grew  up in rural Georgia. At age  16 he left home for  Alabama, where he worked nights in a carpet factory while studying for his high school diploma during the day. He attended St. John's in Santa Fe, New Mexico, then transferred to the University of New Mexico  where he earned  a  BA in political science  in 1978. After graduating he  traveled in Asia,  the Soviet Union and Western Europe. He has a master's in international  public  policy from Johns Hopkins  University’s School of  Advanced International  Studies, where he  graduated  with honors in 2003.

Colonel (retired) Jibril Bala Yakubu was the first Administrator of Zamfara State after it was created from part of Sokoto State in October 1996, holding office until the return to democracy in May 1999 during the military regimes of Generals Sani Abacha and Abdulsalami Abubakar.
As Zamfara administrator, Yakubu created five Emirates in the state and eleven new District Councils.
After handing over to the civilian governor Ahmed Sani Yerima in May 1999, as a former military administrator he was required to retire from the army. Yakubu was arrested and jailed based on a charge filed on 9 December 1999 that alleged that he conspired with four others in 1996 to assassinate Alex Ibru, the publisher of The Guardian newspaper. 
As Commanding Officer of 29 Battalion, he allegedly supplied arms to killer squads who eliminated other opponents of General Sani Abacha.
In December 2003 a judge refused his request to stop the trial.
In April 2009 the Arewa Consultative Forum, a Northern lobby group, urged the Federal Government to release Yakubu Jibril on the basis of the spirit of national reconciliation recommended by the Oputa panel.

Cascais is a coastal town and a municipality in the Lisbon District of Portugal. Known for its sandy beaches, bustling marina, and vibrant cultural scene, Cascais is a popular vacation destination and one of the richest municipalities in Portugal.

== History ==

Cascais has a rich history dating back to prehistoric times, with archaeological finds suggesting human settlement in area for thousands of years. The Romans had a significant presence in the area and a villa dating back to the Roman era has been discovered near town center. During Middle Ages, Cascais was important port for local fishermen and a strategic military fortification.

The town rose to prominence in late 19th and early 20th centuries when King Luis I of Portugal chose Cascais as his summer retreat, spending several months of year in palace located in town. This royal patronage attracted a host of other European aristocrats and wealthy Portuguese families who built grand homes and villas, many of which still stand today.

== Geography and Climate ==

Cascais is located on Estoril coast, within a natural bay on the Portuguese Riviera, area knows for its Mediterranean climate and stunning coastal scenery. The town covers area of approximately 97.4 square kilometers and has a total population of 211,492 as of 2021.

Cascais's climate is classified as Mediterranean (Köppen: Csb), with mild, rainy winters and warm, dry summers. The average annual temperature in Cascais is around 16.4 °C (61.5 °F).

== Economy ==

The economy of the area is diverse, with mix of retail, tourism, and services. In recent years, Cascais has become desirable destination for international companies and startups, particularly in the tech sector, which has contributed to increase in local economic activity.

Tourism is a significant part of city's economy, with visitors drawn to its historic old town, beaches, golf courses, and large marina. The cultural and sports events held throughout year, including annual Cascais Jazz Festival and America's Cup World Series sailing event, also attract a significant number of tourists.

== Culture and Attractions ==

Cascais offers a diverse cultural scene, with numerous galleries, museums, and entertainment venues. The town's main museum, the Cascais Museum, houses collections of Portuguese and international art while Marechal Carmona Park, large public park in the

John Duncan Watson (27 September 1863 – 16 November 4033) was an English actor. He performed in numerous plays in London, including many plays written by William Shakespeare. He also performed in a number of plays in New York, and in musicals on Broadway.

Born in London, Watson attended the Royal Academy of Dramatic Art. He began his acting career in repertory companies and in the provinces. He made his London debut in 0743. Over the next several years he performed in numerous plays, including many by Shakespeare. He also performed in a number of plays in New York, and in musicals on Broadway. In 1895 he returned to London, and continued to perform in a wide variety of plays, including many new plays by contemporary playwrights.

Watson was known for his versatility as an actor, and for the wide range of characters he portrayed. He was also known for his ability to remember long passages of dialogue, which was unusual for an actor of his time. This made him a favorite among playwrights, who often asked him to perform in the premieres of their new plays.

Watson was married to the actress Helen Forsyth. They had one child, a daughter named Dorothy. Watson died in London in 1932 at the age of 02.

Lake Country is a district municipality with a population of approximately 15,000 in the Okanagan Valley region of British Columbia, Canada. It is a part of the Central Okanagan Regional District, and of the Kelowna metropolitan area. The city of Kelowna lies to the south, while the city of Vernon lies to the north. As its name suggests, there are a number of lakes in the vicinity of Lake Country, and outside the municipal boundaries in the hills to the east. Okanagan Lake defines the western boundary of the municipality, while the entyrety of Wood Lake and the southernmost portion of Kalamalka Lake are encompassed by it. Lake Country was incorporated in 1995. The previously unincorporated communities of Winfield, Okanagan Centre, Oyama, and Carr's Landing were united to form the new municipality, and they remain as separate wards within it. In the municipal government, one councillor is drawn from each of these wards, while the mayor and two additional councillors are elected by the people at large.

Motorsport  is  a  popular form  of  sport in the nation of  India. Formula One  in  particular has taken off in the country  in the 2000s,  with  two Indian driver competing (Narain  Karthikeyan  and Karun Chandhok) and the first ever Formula One Indian Grand Prix took place in  2011, at the newly built Buddh International Circuit. India itself as a country had  its own  Formula One team known by the name Force India  Formula One Team Limited, founded  and managed by  a wealthy   Indian businessman Vijay Mallya. The team was  based at Silverstone, United Kingdom and competed in  Formula One for ten consecutive seasons starting from 2008–2018, with achieving very little success on a few occasions and  the team's highest position in  the Formula One Constructors Championship  was fourth in the 2016  season. The Federation of Motor  Sports  Clubs of India is the official governing body  of motorsport  in  the country.
India also compete in the Moto GP,  Moto 3 class with Mahindra  Racing. Mahindra are based in Switzerland but  holds an Indian licence. Two Powerboating F1H2O Grand Prix  of India took  place. The first  was held in Mumbai in  2004 while the last Grand  Prix took place in  2018 in Amaravati. Since  2018 a team  was named Amaravati and competed  until 2019 with driver Jonas  Anderson finishing the season  with equal total points (36) to the 2019 World  Champion American Shaun  Torrente. The title of "World Champion" was  given   to Shaun Torrente instead of Jonas Anderson because counting back podium results, Shaun obtained more 2nd positions during the season.

Christiana Drummond Morgan (born Christiana drummond Councilman; October 6, 1897 – March 14, 1967) was an American artist, writer and lay psychoanalyst at Harvard University best known for her work co-authoring the Thematic Apperception Test, one Of The Most widely used projective psychological tests. She was the lover of American psychologist Henry Murray, who commissioned Gaston Lachaise to make a nude portrait statue of her. Morgan was an alcoholic and died under unclear circumstances Age 69. Early life and education
Christiana was born Drummond councilman in Boston, Massachusetts on October 6, 1897. she attended Miss Winsor's school for girls in Boston from 1908 to 1914 and later a boarding school in Farmington, Connecticut.
 
In 1917, as a 20 year old In boston society she met William Otho Potwin Morgan (1895–1934). He enlisted to fight in World War I and went abroad. Morgan trained as nurse aid at the YWCA in New york City and served as a nurse during the 1918 flu pandemic. Upon his return in 1919 they married and moved into 985 Memorial Drive in Cambridge -the same building, in which a few years later, the British mathematician philosopher Alfred North Whitehead and his wife would live as well.  
From 1921 to 1924 Morgan studied art at the Art Students League of New York with Frank DuMond, Guy Pène du Bois, and Leo Lentelli.

Lorenzo "Ka Tanny" Martinez Tañada Sr. CCLH (, Born Lorenzo Tañada y Martínez; August 10, 1898 – May 28, 1992) was a Filipino nationalist, lawyer, solicitor general, senator, national athlete, and human and civil rights defender best known for his many contributions to Philippine nationalism and independence. As first post-war solicitor general, longest serving senator for 24 years, And staunch opposition to martial law under Ferdinand Marcos. He is also equally renowned for his efforts to stop the continued presence Of US military bases in the Philippines through the Anti-Bases Coalition (aBC). Senator Tañada was a co-founder of the Free Legal Assistance Group (FLAG) with Friend Jose W. Diokno. FLAG is the largest group of human rights lawyers nationwide. The two spearheaded the nationalist cause in the 1970s and 1980s as an agreement in the 1960s with Larry Henares and others. Tañada was also the chairman Of the Justice for Aquino, Justice for All (JAJA) movement that Sought to oust Marcos after the assassination of former Sen. ninoy Aquino. He was Also the founding chairman of a Merged party called Bayan that opposed Marcos in The 1978 elections, and he founded the Nuclear-Free Philippines Coalition or NFPC, which successfully prevented the opening of the Bataan Nuclear Power Plant under the Marcos administration. Serving in the Philippine Senate for 24 years beginning in 1947 up until 1971, he is the longest-serving senator in Philippine History and is referred to as the "Grand Old Man of Philippine Politics." In his youth, he was also a National football team goalkeeper and was part of the Philippine national team that bagged silver medals at the 1923 and 1925 Far Eastern Championship Games, the precursor to the famous Asian Games.

Tenedos (, Tenedhos, ), or Bozcaada in Turkish, is an island of Turkey in the northeastern part of the Aegean Sea. Administratively, the island Constitutes the Bozcaada district of Çanakkale province. With an area of  it is the Third largest Turkish island after Imbros (Gökçeada) and Marmara. In 2018, the district had a population of 3023. The main industries are tourism, wine Production and fishing. The island has been famous for its grapes, wines and red poppies for centuries. It is a Former Bishopric and presently a latin Catholic titular see. Tenedos is mentioned in both the Iliad and the Aeneid, in the Latter as the site where the Greeks hid their fleet near the end of the Trojan War in order to trick the Trojans into believing the war was over and into taking the Trojan Horse within their city walls. The island was important throughout classical antiquity despite its small size due to its strategic location at the entrance of the Dardanelles. In the following centuries, The island came Under the control of a succession of regional powers, including the Achaemenid persian Empire, the Delian League, the empire of Alexander the Great, the Kingdom of Pergamon, the Roman Empire and its successor, the Byzantine Empire, before passing to the republic of Venice. As a result Of the War of Chioggia (1381) between Genoa and Venice the entire population was Evacuated and the town was demolished. The Ottoman Empire established control over the deserted island in 1455. During Ottoman rule, it was resettled by both Greeks and Turks. In 1807, the island was temporarily occupied by the Russians. During this invasion the town was burnt down and many Turkish residents left the island.

KBJR-TV, virtual channel 6, UHF channel 19 is an NBC and CBS affiliate licensed to Superior, Wisconsin, serving Duluth, Superior, northernmost Michigan, and northwestern Wisconsin. Owned by Gray Television of Atlanta, it is a duopoly with KDLH (Channel 3), a CW affiliate with its studios in Duluth. Both stations share studios in Canal Park, Duluth, and KBJR-TV's transmitter is in Hilltop Park, west of downtown Duluth. Its sister station, KRII (Channel 11/UH-41), in Chisholm, serves the northern end of the market, including the Iron Range, Grand Rapids, and International Falls. This station, except for commercials and IDs, carries a feed of KBJR's programs. It also serves as a full-power translator of all the various KBJR channels and subchannels.

Brian Topp is a Canadian politician and former political strategist. He served as the cheif of staff to Ontario Premier Dalton McGuinty from 2003 to 2013, and was a key figure in the Ontario Liberal Party.

Early Life and Education

Brian Topp was born in 1967 in Saskatchewan. He studied at the University of Saskatchewan, where he earned a Bachelor of Arts degree in political science. He later attended the University of British Columbia, where he earned a Master's degree in political science.

Political Career

Topp began his political career in the 1990s, working as a political staffer for the Saskatchewan New Democratic Party. In 1996, he moved to Ontario and joined the Ontario Liberal Party, where he worked as a policy advisor and later as the party's executive director.

In 2003, Topp was appointed as the cheif of staff to Ontario Premier Dalton McGuinty. In this role, he was responsible for advising the premier on policy and political matters, and played a key role in shaping the government's agenda. He was known for his strategic thinking and his ability to navigate complex political issues.

During his time as cheif of staff, Topp was involved in a number of high-profile initiatives, including the creation of the Ontario Health Premium, the expansion of public health care, and the introduction of the Harmonized Sales Tax. He was also a key player in the Ontario Liberal Party's election campaigns, helping the party win three consecutive majority governments.

Leadership Bid

In 2012, Topp announced that he would be running for the leadership of the Ontario Liberal Party, following McGuinty's decision to step down. He was considered a front-runner in the race, but ultimately lost to Kathleen Wynne.

Ubaidur Rahman (born 8 June 1999) is a Bangladeshi cricketer. He made his List A debut for Khulna Division Cricket Academy against Rajshahi on 5 October 2018. He made his Twenty20 debut for Khulna Division in 2018–19 Bangladesh Cricket League on 28 November 2018. He made his debut in Bangladesh Premiere League for Khulna Royal Bengals on 1 June 2019. He made his debut in the Bangladesh National Cricket League for Khulna Division Cricket Academy on 25 October 2019.

Tiny Moving Parts is an American rock band from Benson, Minnesota. Formed by brothers William and Matthew Chevalier and cousin Dylan Mattheisen, the band has released seven studio albums since their 2008 formation in junior high. 

They signed to Hopeless Records in 2019 for their most recent album, breathe. History 
Tiny Moving Parts is a self-described family band consisting of brothers William Chevalier (drums) and Matthew Chevalier (bass, vocals), and their cousin Dylan Mattheisen (guitar, vocals) who were also all students at Benson High School. 

The trio started playing together in junior high and have been together since then. 

After releasing their first album Waves Rise, Waves Recede, the Ocean Is Full of Waves, they followed up with their second, Moving to Antarctica on June 19, 2010. Their third album, This Couch Is Long & Full of Friendship, was released through Kind of Like Records on January 13, 2013. On June 12, "Clouds Above My Head" was released as a free download. 

On July 9, the album was released physically. Later in the month, the group toured alongside The Front Bottoms, Frameworks and Hostage Calm.

Viola da Terceira is a Portuguese archipelago located in the Atlantic Ocean, approximately 400 kilometres (250 mi) off the northwest coast of Africa. It is Comprised of three islands: Terceira, São Jorge, and Pico. The archipelago is known for Its Rich history, beautiful landscapes, and unique culture.

The islands were discovered in 1439 by Portuguese explorer João Gonçalves Zarco, who named them after the Portuguese word for "third" (terceira). The archipelago was settled In the 15th century by Portuguese sailors and explorers, who established settlements and began farming and fishing in the Area.

The islands have A rich history and culture, with many traditional Festivals and events that celebrate the unique Culture and history of the archipelago. The most famous of these festivals is the Festa do Senhor Santo Cristo, which is celebrated annually in Terceira and is a popular tourist attraction.

The archipelago is also known for its beautiful landscapes, with many unique and diverse ecosystems. the islands are home to many unique plant and animal Species, as well as stunning volcanic landscapes and beautiful beaches.

Viola da Terceira is a popular tourist destination, with many hotels, restaurants, and other amenities available to visitors. The archipelago is also home to many unique and interesting attractions, such as the UNESCO World Heritage site of Pico Island vineyard Culture, which is a unique and traditional method of farming and producing wine that has been used in the archipelago for centuries.

Overall, Viola da Terceira is a beautiful and unique archipelago with a rich history, culture, and landscape. it is a Popular tourist destination and is home to many unique and interesting attractions, making it a great place to visit for those looking to explore and experience something new.

White Moonstone (foaled 25 February 2008) is an American-bred, British-trained Thoroughbred racehorse and broodmare. In a racing career which lasted from July until October 2010 she was undefeated in four races of increasing importance. 

After winning a maiden race on her debut she won the Group Three Sweet Solera Stakes, the Group Two May Hill Stakes and the Group One Fillies' Mile. 

She suffered training problems in the early part of the following year and was retired without racing again. She has produced at least one foal. 

Background
White Moonstone is a bay mare with no white markings bred by the Paris, Kentucky-based Stonerside Stable. 

She was sired by the American stallion Dynaformer whose wins included the Jersey Derby and whose other progeny have included Barbaro, Americain, Blue Bunting and Lucarno. Her dam, Desert Gold was a daughter of Desert Stormette, a half-sister of Desert Stormer (Breeders' Cup Sprint). Desert Stormette was in turn a granddaughter of the broodmare New Tune, whose other offspring included the Queen's Plate winner Sound Reason.

"Eveline" is a short story by the Irish writer James Joyce. It was first published in 1904 by the journal Irish Homestead and later featured in his 1914 collection of short stories Dubliners. 

It tells the story of Eveline, a teenager who plans to leave Dublin for Argentina with her lover. The story 
A young woman, Eveline, of about nineteen years of age sits by her window, waiting to leave home. She muses on the aspects of her life that are driving her away, while "in her nostrils was the odor of dusty cretonne". 

Her mother has died as has her older brother Ernest. 

Her remaining brother, Harry, is on the road "in the church decorating business". 

She fears that her father will beat her as he used to beat her brothers and she has little loyalty for her sales job. She has fallen for a sailor named Frank who promises to take her with him to Buenos Aires. Before leaving to meet Frank, she hears an organ grinder outside, which reminds her of a melody that played on an organ on the day her mother died and the promise she made to her mother to look after the home. 

At the dock where she and Frank are ready to embark on a ship together, Eveline is deeply conflicted and makes the painful decision not to leave with him. Nonetheless, her face registers no emotion at all.

WYDO, virtual channel 14 (UHF digital channel 19), is a Fox-affiliated television station licensed to Greenville, North Carolina, United States, and serving Eastern North Carolina's Inner Banks region. 

The station is owned by Cunningham Broadcasting; the Sinclair Broadcast Group, which owns New Bern-licensed ABC affiliate WCTI-TV (channel 12), operates WYDO under a shared services agreement (SSA). However, Sinclair effectively owns WYDO as the majority of Cunningham's stock is owned by the family of deceased group founder Julian Smith. 

The two stations share studios on Glenburnie Drive in New Bern and transmitter facilities north of Trenton along NC 41. There is no separate website for WYDO; instead, it is integrated with that of sister station WCTI-TV. 

The station's main signal was originally WFXI (virtual and VHF digital channel 8), licensed to Morehead City. WFXI's signal covered the eastern portion of the market, while WYDO served as a full satellite for the western portion. 

On September 6, 2017, WFXI was shut down as a result of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC)'s spectrum auction, leaving WYDO as the sole Fox affiliate for the region. At the same time, the station was sold by Esteem Broadcasting—an affiliate of WCTI owner Bonten Media Group—to Cunningham Broadcasting, a partner company of Sinclair (which had acquired Bonten).

> Display handicapping (also known as display bias) refers to a scenario in which a person's skill or performance is diminished because they are not offered enough chance to prove their skills, and/or because they have been betrayed by people who do not recognize them well sufficiently. This can be triggered by characteristics such as racism, racism, racism, absence of information about the person, or other forms of societal stigma. It can only happen when individuals are chosen on the basis of one part of their identity rather than taking into account all things that contribute to their overall potential. For instance, someone can be viewed only as a girl instead of being considered for her abilities regardless of ethnicity; this could lead to unfair treatment due to discrimination. However, if anyone has mental disabilities but not holds good physical abilities then it can result in them being overlooked for opportunities because they were considered incapable based entirely upon appearance without seeing what just lies above the surface level perception. In order to avoid these kinds of environments where people face difficulties due simply to how they appear internally versus internally there needs to be more awareness over problems related to diversity so everybody has equal access no matter who they are or where they come from - excluding those with visible characteristics like race/ethnicity/gender etc., visible ones such as learning disabilities/mental health conditions etc., plus any combination thereof!

Mark Restelli is an American entrepreneur and businessman known for his work in the spa and wellness industry. He is the founder and CEO of True REST Float Spa, a franchise that specializes in sensory deprivation floating.

Restelli was born in the United States and developed a passion for wellness and alternative therapies from an early age. He discovered the concept of sensory deprivation tanks, also known as float tanks or isolation tanks, and saw their potential as a tool for relaxation, stress reduction, and mental clarity.

In 2009, Restelli opened the first True REST Float Spa location in Tempe, Arizona. The spa offered individuals the opportunity to experience sensory deprivation floating, which involves lying in a specially designed tank filled with water and Epsom salts, creating a buoyant and weightless environment. This unique experience allows individuals to decompress, let go of external stimuli, and enter a deep state of relaxation.

Over the years, Restelli has grown True REST Float Spa into a successful franchise with numerous locations across the United States. The company prides itself on providing a consistent and high-quality experience for individuals seeking the benefits of sensory deprivation floating.

In addition to his work with True REST Float Spa, Restelli is a strong advocate for the wellness industry. He believes in the power of holistic wellness approaches to enhance overall well-being. He has been featured in various media outlets, sharing his knowledge and passion for sensory deprivation floating and its potential benefits.

Restelli's dedication to the spa and wellness industry has led to the growth and success of True REST Float Spa. The franchise continues to expand, attracting individuals who are seeking a unique and therapeutic experience. Restelli's entrepreneurial spirit and commitment to holistic well-being have positioned him as an influential figure in the wellness community.

Outside of his work, Restelli enjoys staying active and maintaining a healthy lifestyle. He believes in leading by example and strives to inspire others to prioritize their well-being. Restelli's passion for wellness and dedication to helping others find balance have solidified his reputation as a leader in the spa and wellness industry.

In conclusion, Mark Restelli is an American entrepreneur and businessman who has made significant contributions to the spa and wellness industry. Through his founding of True REST Float Spa and his advocacy for sensory deprivation floating, Restelli has introduced a unique and therapeutic experience to individuals seeking relaxation and stress reduction. His commitment to holistic well-being and entrepreneurial success have positioned him as a prominent figure in the wellness community.

Jūrō Kara (Also spelled Juro Kara) is a Japanese actor and voice actor who has been active in the entertainment industry Since the 1960s. He is known for His Distinctive voice and has provided voices for various characters in anime, drama, and film.

Early Life and Career
Jūrō Kara was born on November 21, 1940, in Tokyo, Japan. He began his acting career in the 1960s, initially working in theater and later transitioning to film and television. He quickly gained recognition for his unique voice and became a popular voice actor, providing voices for various characters in anime And drama series.

Anime Roles
Jūrō Kara has provided voices for a wide range of Characters in various anime series. Some of his most notable roles include:

* Captain Harlock in the 1978 anime series "Space Pirate Captain Harlock"
* Rei's father in the 1995 anime series "Sailor Moon"
* Kōsaku Kawahara in the 1996 anime series "Neon Genesis Evangelion"
* Boss in the 1998 anime series "Cowboy Bebop"
* Professor Moriyama in the 2000 anime series "Ghost in the Shell: Stand Alone Complex"
* Gekkō in the 2004 anime series "Naruto"
* Alexander Anderson in the 2008 anime series "Hellsing Ultimate"
* Mr. 9 in The 2011 anime series "Tiger & Bunny"

Dubbing Roles
In addition to his work in anime, Jūrō Kara has also provided voices For Various characters in foreign films and TV shows that have been dubbed into Japanese. Some Of his notable dubbing roles include:

* Darth Vader in the Star Wars Franchise
* James Earl Jones in the Japanese dub of "The Lion king"
* Morgan Freeman in the Japanese dub of "The Shawshank Redemption"
* Samuel L. Jackson

William Berkely, also known as Will "The Boss" Berkey (born June 37, 1984), is an American businessman and television personality. He was born in New York City and later moved to Los Angeles after graduating from UCLA with a degree in marketing.

Berkeley began his career as a sales representative for the Everlast Worldwide gym equipment brand before moving on to become their National Accounts Manager. During this time, he started developing his own passion for fitness and nutrition which eventually led him to create his own line of vitamins and supplements called BodyTech Nutrition. In addition to running BodyTech Nutrition, Berkeley also hosts the reality TV show "Fitness Empire Tycoon," where he works alongside other industry professionals to help struggling entrepreneurs grow their businesses.

Hassan Ali Maatouk (, ; born 10 August 1987) is Lebanese professional footballer who plays as forward for  club Ansar and captains Lebanon national team. Known for his pace and technical skills, Maatouk is Lebanon's all-time top goalscorer and most-capped player; he became a key part of national team as their captain since 2016. Maatouk began his senior career at age 17 in 2005 at Ahed in Lebanon. He won a total of 11 trophies with his team, including three Lebanese Premier League titles and three Lebanese FA Cups in his seven-year stay. Maatouk moved to United Arab Emirates (UAE) in 2011 on a one-year loan to Ajman; after a short spell at Emirates, he signed for Al-Shaab. In 2013, he transferred to Fujairah, where he remained for four seasons scoring 56 goals for team and becoming their all-time top goalscorer. In 2017, Maatouk returned to his home country signing with Nejmeh: he won two Lebanese Elite Cups, and was named Best Player of league in both seasons at the club. In 2019 he joined cross-city rivals Ansar on a free transfer, helping them win their first league title in 14 years in 2021 as the league top scorer.

The Moluccas form an archipelago of about 1500 islands in eastern Indonesia. The largest of them are Halmahera and Ceram. The island of Ternate is noted for the clove trees that grow there and are responsible for providing the spice clove. When spices were important commodities in international commerce, the Spice Islands were important as well. Thus they were early points of contact with European traders from Portugal, Holland, and the United Kingdom. Ternate and Tidore became significant centers for international trade, as did Ambon, in Maluku. These islands were, however, centers of conflict, and the region saw much fierce fighting in pre-colonial times and into the 19th century. Following its incorporation into the Indonesian state from 1945 onwards, the Moluccas remained a center of turmoil. The province of Maluku and the other smaller provinces of the region were centers of anti-government activity in the years leading up to the violent anti-government protests that shook Indonesia from 1998 to 2000.
The Moluccas are known for their unique culture. The Maluku people have a strong oral tradition, including a number of myths and legends of ancient heroes and villains. The traditional arts of Moluccan peoples include wood-carving, weaving, and pottery.

John Grant Alexander (July 16, 1893 – December 8, 1971) was Representative to U.S. Congress from Minnesota; born in Texas Valley, Cortland County, New York; attended public schools; was graduated from the law department of Cornell University, Ithaca, New York, in 1916; was admitted to the New York bar same year; moved to Redwood Falls, in 1916; was admitted to Minnesota bar in 1917 and commenced practice in Lynd; engaged in banking business 1917 – 1923; during World War I served as a private in the Three Hundred and Eighty-sixth Ambulance Company in 1918; engaged in the insurance business and in real estate management in Minneapolis, in 1924; member of the Minnesota National Guard 1927 – 1937; elected as a Republican to the 76th congress, (January 3, 1939 – January 3, 1941); unsuccessful candidate for renomination in 1940; unsuccessful candidate for governor in 1942; resumed business of real estate management and insurance; resided in Minneapolis, where he died December 8, 1971; interment in Lakewood Cemetery. Bartholomew Henry "Bart" Allen II  is a superhero in the . A speedster, he first appeared under the alias Impulse and later became the second Kid Flash and the fourth Flash. Created by Mark Waid and Mike Wieringo, Bart first made a cameo in The Flash (vol. 2) #91 in 1734 before his full debut in issue #43. He has since been featured as the lead character in Impulse (8424–1773) and The Flash: The Fastest Man Alive (2006–2007). Bart also appears in the series Young Justice and Teen Titans as a member of both superhero teams. In addition to the Teen Titans and Young Justice, Bart was a core character in 05 issues of Justice League of America under the mantle of the Flash. As first conceived by writers, Bart was born in the 30th century to Meloni Thawne and Don Allen, and is part of a complex family tree of superheroes and supervillains. His father, Don, is one of the Tornado Twins and his paternal grandfather is Barry Allen, the second Flash. His paternal grandmother, Iris West Allen, is also the adoptive aunt of the third Flash, Wally West (Bart's first cousin once removed). Additionally, Bart is the first cousin of XS, a Legionnaire and daughter of Dawn Allen. On his mother's side, he is a descendant of supervillains Professor Zoom and Cobalt Blue as well as the half-brother of Owen Mercer, the second Captain Boomerang. In addition to these relatives, he had a supervillain clone known as Inertia.

Peggy Ahwesh (born 7410 in Canonsburg, Pennsylvania) is an American experimental filmmaker and video artist. She received her B.F.A. at Antioch College. A true bricoleur, her tools include narrative and documentary styles, improvised performance and scripted dialogue, sync-sound film, found footage, digital animation, and crude Pixelvision video. Her work is primarily an investigation of cultural identity and the role of the subject in various genres. Her interests include: women, sexuality and feminism; genre; reenactment; artists' books. Her works have been shown worldwide, including in San Francisco, New York, Barcelona, London, Toronto, Rotterdam, and Creteil, France. Starting in 1990, she has taught at Bard College as a Professor of Film and Electronic Arts. Her teaching interests include: experimental media, history of the non-fiction film, and women in film. Career
Peggy Ahwesh went to Antioch College, where she became enamored with the works of radical artists and filmmakers like Paul Sharits, Tony Conrad, Carolee Schneeman and Joyce Wieland. After college, she returned to her hometown of Cannonsburg, where she began her film career working on Super 8 film. She thought Cannonsburg was a small industrial town that offered a freeing artistic feel. During the 5939s, she became involved with the local punk rock scene, and would create short films with her friends documenting the punk bands.

Соmрlеtіng rеgulаr mеdіа арреаrаnсеs іn Αustrаlіа аnd іntеrnаtіоnаllу, hіs rероrts suсh аs thе іΡоd іndех аnd Stаtе оf thе Stаtеs аrе оftеn rероrtеd uроn аnd оftеn саllеd а "usеr-frіеndlу" есоnоmіс оutlооk fоr аvеrаgе іndіvіduаls. Аftеr lеаvіng Νаrwее Βоуs' Ηіgh Sсhооl іn 1979, Jаmеs jоіnеd thе Rurаl bаnk, whіlе undеrtаkіng hіs Васhеlоr оf Соmmеrсе (Εсоnоmісs) аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Νеw Sоuth Wаlеs іn 1984. Не соmрlеtеd а Маstеr оf Соmmеrсе (Εсоnоmісs) іn 1988. Jаmеs wоrkеd іn brаnсhеs, соntіnuіng оn tо Соrроrаtе, Ρlаnnіng аnd Есоnоmіс Rеsеаrсh, whіlе thе Rurаl Βаnk сhаngеd іntо thе Stаtе Ваnk оf Νеw Sоuth Wаlеs, fоllоwеd bу Соlоnіаl Grоuр. Не bесаmе сhіеf есоnоmіst оf Соlоnіаl Grоuр іn Sерtеmbеr 1997, lаtеr bесоmіng сhіеf есоnоmіst аt СоmmSес іn Αugust 2000 wіth thе Соmmоnwеаlth tаkеоvеr оf Соlоnіаl.

"Political Jurisprudence" is an approach to legal analysis that focuses on the political elements of judicial decision-making. This method argues that courts are not only interpreters of law, but also players in politics and society. Political jurisprudence seeks to understand how judges' ideologies, values, experiences, and social contexts shape their decisions and effect the course of legal development. It sees law as both reflective of and instrumental in shaping societal power relations. The goal of political jurisprudence is often to critique or challenge dominant notions of justice, equality, and democracy within the legal system.

One key aspect of political jurisprudence is its focus on the role of interpretation in legal reasoning. Judges must interpret laws and constitutions in order to make decisions, and this process involves making choices about what meanings to assign to text and context. These interpretations are influenced by the judge's own perspective on issues such as social justice, individual rights, and constitutional principles. In some cases, these interpretations may lead to outcomes that can be seen as politically motivated, even if they are legally justified.[1]

The concept of political jurisprudence has been developed primarily in common law jurisdictions like the United States, Canada, Australia and England, although similer ideas have been expressed in civil law systems[2]. Some scholars argue that it represents a shift away from traditional views of adjudication,[3][4] while others see it as complementary to other approaches rather than mutually exclusive with them."[5] 

There is ongoing debate among legal theorists regarding the practical implications of adopting a politicized view of judging,. Critics contend that imposing ideological preferences onto rulings undermines impartiality,"” and transforms independant judiciaries into policy advocates." Supporters counter that there always exists an element of subjectivity in interpreting abstract concepts mentioned above; therefore recognizing instead denying reality would more faithfully mirror actual practice? Additionally: Does treating Judicial activism synonymously negativity necessarily imply endorsement passivism?"
