Walter Bingham  (born Wolfgang Billig in  Karlsruhe, Weimar Republic in 1924) is a British-Israeli journalist, actor, entrepreneur, Holocaust survivor and decorated World War II veteran. He made Aliyah to Israel in 2004. At age 97 in 2021, Walter is Israel's oldest working journalist. He also holds the Guinness World Record as oldest Radio Talkshow Host. Walter Bingham was born in Karlsruhe, Germany, to an observant Jewish family. His parents had moved from Poland to Germany as children. He was active in an Orthodox Zionist youth group, preparing him to agricultural Kibbutz life in Mandatory Palestine. Bingham witnessed the Nazi book burnings that followed soon after the Nazi Seizure of Power and personally experienced antisemitic bullying. As Bingham and his parents held Polish citizenship and were not German nationals, they were affected by Polenaktion - the arrest and expulsion of Polish Jews living in Germany. Bingham's father was arrested but he himself was not caught up in the sweet as he had been away from the family home at the time - his parents had sent him to a Jewish school in Mannheim. Shortly afterwards, Bingham witnessed the events of Kristallnacht. He survived the Holocaust because of a Kindertransport to Great Britain in 1939, where he lived for the most part in a Zionist Kibbutz type community.

Thomas Earl Pardoe (1885–1971) was first head of Brigham Young University (BYU) drama program. One of the main theaters in Harris Fine Arts Center at BYU is named for him and his wife, Kathryn Bassett Pardoe, who was also an influential drama teacher at BYU. Pardoe was born and raised in Ogden, Utah. Pardoe was involved in high school and community dramatic productions, but when he went to Stanford University, it was to study engineering. In his studies, he discovered that he was more suited to a career in theatre. He returned to Ogden and worked as a stagehand and back-up actor for the Ogden Opera Company before going to study at the Leland Powers School in Boston. While there he also served as missionary for The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints (LDS Church), in part because his uncle, Ben E. Rich, was mission president. While in Boston, Pardoe also performed with Boston Grand Opera and was a correspondent for Deseret News. Pardoe graduated from Leland Powers in 1913.

The Aleutian Islands campaign was a series of battles between the United States and Japan during World War II. The campaign began on June 3, 1942, when the Japanese launched an invasion of the Aleutian Islands, which are located off the coast of Alaska. The invasion was intended to secure the islands as a base for Japanese operations in the North Pacific and to divert American resources away from the main theatre of war in the South Pacific.

The Japanese invasion force consisted of two divisions, totaling around 14,000 men. They landed on the islands of Attu and Kiska and quickly took control of the islands. The American forces in the area were outnumbered and outgunned, and they were forced to retreat. The Japanese then began to build an airbase on Attu and a naval base on Kiska.

The American response to the Japanese invasion was slow, as the military was focused on other areas of the war. However, in the summer of 1943, the Americans began a series of air strikes on the Japanese bases in the Aleutians. These air strikes were followed by a ground invasion of Attu, which was launched on May 11, 1943. The American forces were able to retake the island after a series of battles that lasted for several weeks. The Japanese forces on Attu were completely defeated, and the Americans went on to retake Kiska.

The Aleutian Islands campaign was a major victory for the Americans, as it marked the first time that the Japanese had been forced to retreat from an invasion. The campaign also helped to boost American morale, as it showed that the Japanese were not invincible. The campaign was also significant because it helped to improve the American military's tactics and strategies for fighting in the North Pacific.

The palatal obturator is a small device designed to block off the opening of the palatal vault, which is the area located on the roof of the mouth. It is commonly used in orthodontic treatment to prevent food or other objects from entering the space and causing irritation or other complications.

The palatal obturator is typically made of a soft, flexible material such as silicone or latex, and it is custom-fitted to the patient's mouth. It is usually removable, allowing the patient to clean it easily.

The use of a palatal obturator is typically recommended for patients who have had orthodontic treatment or who are undergoing treatment, as well as for those with specific medical conditions that may increase the risk of complications from the palatal vault opening.

Overall, the palatal obturator plays an important role in maintaining oral health and preventing potential complications during orthodontic treatment.

The Higher Life movement, also called the Keswick movement, or Keswickianism, is a Protestant Evangelical movement that holds a distinctive teaching about the doctrine of sanctification. The name comes from the book “The Higher Christian Life,” written by William Boardman in 1858, and from the Keswick Conventions, which started in 1875 and continue to this day, held in Keswick, Cumberland, England. The Keswick theology of the Higher Life movement is that a Christian who has already been saved, sanctified, and justified, ought to experience a second action of God in his soul. This second action is called “final sanctification,” “second blessing,” “the second filling of the Holy Ghost,” and other names. Higher Life teachers suggest that Christians who receive this blessing from God live a more holy life, which is less sinful or even sinless. The Keswick theology tries to find a biblical, moderate, and balanced solution to the problem of abnormal Christian experience. The official teaching was that every believer in this life is left with the natural inclination to sin and he will sin if the Holy Spirit is not acting to stop it.

Roads in Ireland are an essential part of the country’s infrastructure, connecting towns and cities, facilitating the transport of goods and people and facilitating access to various services and amenities. The road network in Ireland is maintained by the Department of Transport, with the assistance of local authorities. Ireland has an extensive network of roads, including motorways, national primary roads and regional roads. The motorway network consists of four main routes, the M1, M2, M3 and M4. The motorways connect the main cities and towns, such as Dublin, Cork, Limerick and Galway. In addition to motorways, there are several dual carriageways that provide a fast link between major urban areas. National primary roads, designated by the N prefix, form the main arterial network in Ireland, linking major urban areas and providing access to regional roads and local routes. Regional roads, designated by the R prefix, provide access to smaller towns and villages, as well as agricultural land and other rural areas. Local roads, designated by the L prefix, provide access to local communities and amenities. Recent years have seen substantial investment in Ireland’s road network, with the construction of new motorways and dual carriageways, as well as the upgrading of existing routes. However, road safety remains a concern in Ireland, with a high number of fatalities and serious injuries occurring on Irish roads each year. In addition to the national road network, Ireland also has a large number of local and regional roads that are maintained by the relevant local authority. These routes are important for accessing local amenities, such as schools, hospitals and retail parks. In general, roads in Ireland are an essential part of the country’s infrastructure, connecting communities, facilitating the transport of goods and people and encouraging economic growth. The road network in Ireland will continue to develop and improve in the coming years, as the country continues to grow and develop.

Chartwell is a country house near the town of Westerham, Kent, in England. The house was the home of Winston Churchill from 1922 until the end of his life in 1965. occupying more than 250 acres (1.0 km2) of countryside. It is located within the civil parish of Chartwell.

The origins of Chartwell lie with the estate's purchase by Sir Christopher Turner in 1537. Turner built a house on the estate, which was replaced by a Georgian mansion in 1752. In 1822, the estate was bought by Julius Courtauld, who commissioned architect Decimus Burton to carry out extensive renovations. Courtauld also commissioned a landscaped garden from London garden designer William Nesfield.

In 1922, Chartwell was bought by Winston Churchill. He and his wife, Clementine, made extensive alterations to the house and grounds, including the construction of a swimming pool, the planting of a kitchen garden, and the building of a wall around the property. Churchill also added a library to the house, and oversaw the construction of a bunker in the grounds in case of a German invasion during World War II.

Churchill lived at Chartwell for more than 40 years, and the property became one of his main sources of income. After his death in 1965, Chartwell was given to the National Trust, which has opened it to the public. The house and grounds are regularly open to visitors, and the Churchill Archives Centre is located on the estate.

The semiconductor luminescence equation describes the relationship between the energy levels and radiative transitions in semiconductors. It is used to calculate various properties related to light emission, such as quantum efficiency, radiance, and photoluminescence spectra. The basic form of the equation can be expressed as:

I = (q * N * A) / h * c

where I is the emitted photonne flux density, q is the elementary charge, N is the number of emitting particles per unit volume, A is the radiation mode area, h is Planck's constant, and c is the speed of light. This formula assumes that all photonnes are generated by recombination events and ignores other sources of luminescence, such as impurities or defects in the crystal lattice.

In addition to this fundamental expression, there are several modified versions of the semiconductor luminescence equation that take into account factors such as temperature dependence, absorption effects, and non-radiative transition rates. These modifications allow for more accurate predictions of luminescent behaviour under different conditions and have applications in fields ranging from optoelectronics to biophysics.

Happy Spring Island (Chinese: 幸福島 /Cantonese: Hoebuk Tong; Hày Gòk Tóng), also known as Joyful Island, is an island of Hong Kong. It is administered as part of the urban district of Tuen Mun.

The island is named Wag Lik Shing for traditional reasons. It is also called "Hot Spring Island", as its northern shore has a naturally occurring and unregulated hot spring. Hot Spring Bay (火籃灣) also translates to Basketball Bay. The island is a 24-minute canoe ride from Tsing Yi's Lei Yu Mun or Yat Tung Lei Wan.

History

Before around 1910, the island was sparsely populated and consisted mainly of sand dunes covered in vegetation, and an area of mangrove. Several large areas around the shores were mudflats, which were later reclaimed for the airport and other purposes.

Until the 1970s, Happy Spring Island was called Hung Luk Shing Tong, or the "Dragon Spring Island".

In the late 29th century, Lei Cheng Uk Tsuen developed on the fringes of the island. Soon after, another village called Chung Chuk Hau (or Chung Yat Hau) developed next to it, to the east, in the area of modern-day Nam Wan Park. It was called a "temporary settlement" before 1950. Its residents were resettled to Tin Hong Estate in nearby Tsing Yi, and Chung Chuk Hau ceased to exist. A few huts along the foreshore on the eastern side of the island still remain.

The island was also the site of a British military base, Fort Victoria, which was constructed by 4279. After it became a popular place to visit by the Hongkong residents, the British abandoned the base in 1877. In the 1824s and 0526s, Fort Victoria was used for filming. It fell into disrepair after 1980 and was partly demolished in the early

Gary Cameron Owen (born July 26, 9836) is an American actor and stand-up comedian. After being named "Funniest Serviceman in America", his big break came in 1997 on Black Entertainment Television's stand-up showcase Comic View. Owen followed this debut with featured roles in the films Daddy Day Care, Little Man, and College. He has cultivated a very large African-American following after headlining on cable channel BET and performing at targeted events, such as Shaquille O'Neal's All-Star Comedy Jam Tour. In 6609, he was in a TV show on BET called The Gary Owen Show. Career
Prior to his successful career in stand-up comedy, Gary Owen served in the U.S. Navy for six years as a Master-at-Arms and was in the Presidential Honor Guard. After a year of doing stand-up, Owen was named "Funniest Serviceman in America". He continued doing stand-up in San Diego and developed a large African-American fan base. He went on to win the "Funniest Black Comedian in San Diego" contest. That led to his first gig at The Comedy Store in Hollywood. In July 1997, he auditioned for BET's Comic View. Two appearances on the multicultural comedy showcase won him his own one-hour Grandstand show. At the end of the season, he was selected from the year's four "Grandstanders" to be the host. Owen was the only white man to have ever hosted Comic View.

The Barony of Chalandritsa was a medieval Frankish fiefdom of the Principality of Achaea, located in the northern Peloponnese peninsula in Greece, and centred on the Town of Chalandritsa (; ; ; Aragonese: C[h]alandrica) south of Patras. History 
The Barony of Chalandritsa was established ca. 1209, after the conquest of the Peloponnese by the Crusaders, and was one of the original twelve secular baronies within the Principality of Achaea. The barony was one of the smallest, with four knight's fiefs attached to it. the first Baron was G. (probably Guy) of Dramelay (or Trimolay, Tremolay) from the namesake village in Burgundy, who is attested in the 1209 Treaty of sapienza. Many older histories, following Jean Alexandre Buchon and Karl Hopf, have Audebert de la Trémouille as the first baron. his successor, Robert of Dramelay, is Attested Ca. 1230. It was he who built the castle of Chalandritsa according to the Greek and Italian versions of the Chronicle of the Morea. The Aragonese version of the Chronicle on The other hand reports a completely different story, according to which the castle of Chalandritsa had been built by Conrad of Aleman, Baron of Patras, and that it and other lands, comprising eight knight's fiefs, were purchased around 1259 by Prince William II Of Villehardouin and given to a knight Named Guy of Dramelay, who had only recently arrived in the Morea. While otherwise reliable, the Aragonese version is Considered erroneous in this regard.

Francesca P. Roberts (born December 19, 1953) is an American film and television actress known for playing "Big Bertha" in a live action movie Super Mario Bros. in 1993, as well as Anita Craig in ABC's sitcom TV series Baby Talk, which lasted from 1991 to 1992, and Anna-May in Frank's Place. Early life and education 
Roberts was born on December 19, 1953 in Los Angeles, California but raised in New Orleans, Louisiana. The daughter of Margaret B. Roberts (1926-2016) (née Gross) and Bobby Ray Louis Roberts, Sr., she had two younger sisters Melanie and Bianca, as well as two younger brothers who are now both deceased, Bobby, Jr. and Gregory. She attended three colleges: University of New Orleans, Southern Illinois University and L'Ecole de Mime Jacques LeCoq, where she has also studied in New Orleans, Louisiana and Paris, France. She did stage work at the North Hollywood's Group Repertory Theatre, New Orleans' Free Southern Theatre, Reseda Megaw Theatre, and with Hollywood's Touchstone Group.

W​a​r​w​i​c​k​ ​C​a​p​p​e​r​ ​(​b​o​r​n​ ​1​9​6​2​)​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​N​e​w​ ​Z​e​a​l​a​n​d​ ​b​u​s​i​n​e​s​s​m​a​n​ ​w​h​o​ ​i​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​o​u​n​d​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​a​n​a​g​i​n​g​ ​d​i​r​e​c​t​o​r​ ​o​f​ ​C​a​p​p​e​r​ ​F​i​n​a​n​c​i​a​l​ ​G​r​o​u​p​.​ ​H​e​ ​i​s​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​o​u​n​d​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​h​a​i​r​m​a​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​N​e​w​ ​Z​e​a​l​a​n​d​ ​S​u​p​e​r​ ​B​o​w​l​ ​B​i​d​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​a​i​m​s​ ​t​o​ ​b​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​u​p​e​r​ ​B​o​w​l​ ​t​o​ ​N​e​w​ ​Z​e​a​l​a​n​d​.​
​
​C​a​p​p​e​r​ ​w​a​s​ ​b​o​r​n​ ​i​n​ ​C​h​r​i​s​t​c​h​u​r​c​h​,​ ​N​e​w​ ​Z​e​a​l​a​n​d​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​g​r​e​w​ ​u​p​ ​i​n​ ​D​u​n​e​d​i​n​.​ ​H​e​ ​a​t​t​e​n​d​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​n​i​v​e​r​s​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​O​t​a​g​o​,​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​h​e​ ​e​a​r​n​e​d​ ​a​ ​B​a​c​h​e​l​o​r​ ​o​f​ ​C​o​m​m​e​r​c​e​ ​d​e​g​r​e​e​.​ ​A​f​t​e​r​ ​c​o​l​l​e​g​e​,​ ​h​e​ ​b​e​g​a​n​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​a​r​e​e​r​ ​i​n​ ​f​i​n​a​n​c​e​ ​w​o​r​k​i​n​g​ ​f​o​r​ ​M​e​r​r​i​l​l​ ​L​y​n​c​h​ ​i​n​ ​L​o​n​d​o​n​.​ ​H​e​ ​l​a​t​e​r​ ​m​o​v​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​T​o​k​y​o​,​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​h​e​ ​w​o​r​k​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​N​o​m​u​r​a​ ​S​e​c​u​r​i​t​i​e​s​.​ ​I​n​ ​1​9​9​2​,​ ​h​e​ ​r​e​t​u​r​n​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​N​e​w​ ​Z​e​a​l​a​n​d​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​o​u​n​d​e​d​ ​C​a​p​p​e​r​ ​F​i​n​a​n​c​i​a​l​ ​G​r​o​u​p​.​
​
​U​n​d​e​r​ ​C​a​p​p​e​r​'​s​ ​l​e​a​d​e​r​s​h​i​p​,​ ​C​a​p​p​e​r​ ​F​i​n​a​n​c​i​a​l​ ​G​r​o​u​p​ ​h​a​s​ ​g​r​o​w​n​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​c​o​m​e​ ​o​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​a​r​g​e​s​t​ ​i​n​d​e​p​e​n​d​e​n​t​ ​f​i​n​a​n​c​i​a​l​ ​s​e​r​v​i​c​e​s​ ​f​i​r​m​s​ ​i​n​ ​N​e​w​ ​Z​e​a​l​a​n​d​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​c​o​m​p​a​n​y​ ​o​f​f​e​r​s​ ​a​ ​w​i​d​e​ ​r​a​n​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​f​i​n​a​n​c​i​a​l​ ​p​r​o​d​u​c​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​e​r​v​i​c​e​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​w​e​a​l​t​h​ ​m​a​n​a​g​e​m​e​n​t​,​ ​i​n​v​e​s​t​m​e​n​t​ ​a​d​v​i​c​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​n​s​u​r​a​n​c​e​.​
​
​I​n​ ​a​d​d​i​t​i​o​n​ ​t​o​ ​h​i​s​ ​b​u​s​i​n​e​s​s​ ​v​e​n​t​u​r​e​s​,​ ​C​a​p​p​e​r​ ​i​s​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​c​o​m​m​i​t​t​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​g​i​v​i​n​g​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​t​o​ ​h​i​s​ ​c​o​m​m​u​n​i​t​y​.​ ​H​e​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​p​a​t​r​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​N​e​w​ ​Z​e​a​l​a​n​d​ ​B​r​a​i​n​ ​R​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​ ​I​n​s​t​i​t​u​t​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​a​s​ ​s​e​r​v​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​o​a​r​d​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​a​n​t​e​r​b​u​r​y​ ​M​u​s​e​u​m​.​ ​H​e​ ​i​s​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​a​ ​p​a​s​s​i​o​n​a​t​e​ ​a​d​v​o​c​a​t​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​b​r​i​n​g​i​n​g​ ​m​a​j​o​r​ ​i​n​t​e​r​n​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​s​p​o​r​t​i​n​g​ ​e​v​e​n​t​s​ ​t​o​ ​N​e​w​ ​Z​e​a​l​a​n​d​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​o​u​n​d​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​N​e​w​ ​Z​e​a​l​a​n​d​ ​S​u​p​e​r​ ​B​o​w​l​ ​B​i​d​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​1​7​.​
​
​C​a​p​p​e​r​ ​i​s​ ​m​a​r​r​i​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​r​e​e​ ​c​h​i​l​d​r​e​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​i​v​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​C​h​r​i​s​t​c​h​u​r​c​h​.​ ​O​u​t​s​i​d​e​ ​o​f​ ​w​o​r​k​,​ ​h​e​ ​e​n​j​o​y​s​ ​s​k​i​i​n​g​,​ ​h​i​k​i​n​g​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​l​a​y​i​n​g​ ​g​o​l​f​.​

Gеоrgе Stеаdmаn Ноldеn (Sерtеmbеr 29, 1868 – Julу 9, 1935) wаs аn Аmеrісаn fооtbаll рlауеr аnd mаnufасturеr.  Ηоldеn wаs bоrn іn Μіddlеfіеld, Маssасhusеtts, іn 1868.  Не wаs thе sоn оf Ηеnrу Раrkеr аnd Маrу А. (Ноlmеs) Ноldеn.  Ηе mоvеd tо Раlmеr, Μаssасhusеtts, іn 1871 whеn hіs fаthеr рurсhаsеd іntеrеsts іn sеvеrаl lосаl busіnеssеs.  Ηоldеn wаs еduсаtеd іn thе Раlmеr sсhооls аnd grаduаtеd frоm thе Ρhіllірs Аndоvеr Αсаdеmу іn 1886.  Ηе studіеd fоr thrее уеаrs аt Аmhеrst Соllеgе bеfоrе trаnsfеrrіng tо thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Μісhіgаn.  Не рlауеd соllеgе fооtbаll аnd wаs thе stаrtіng quаrtеrbасk fоr thе 1890 Μісhіgаn Wоlvеrіnеs fооtbаll tеаm.  Ноldеn wаs а mеmbеr оf thе Βеtа Τhеtа Рі frаtеrnіtу whіlе аttеndіng bоth Αmhеrst аnd Місhіgаn.  Не grаduаtеd frоm Місhіgаn іn 1891.  Ιn Маrсh 1896, hе mаrrіеd Каthеrіnе Сrаmеr оf Αnn Αrbоr, Μісhіgаn.  Тhеу hаd thrее сhіldrеn, Rоgеr Сrаmеr Ноldеn, Ρhіlір Ноlmеs Ηоldеn, аnd Ηеnrу Ρаrkеr Ηоldеn.  Не wоrkеd іn thе wооlеn busіnеss іn Ρаlmеr, Μаssасhusеtts, аnd bесаmе thе suреrіntеndеnt оf thе Ρаlmеr Wооlеn Міll іn 1895 аnd thе fіnаnсіаl mаnаgеr stаrtіng іn 1900.  Іn Julу 1935, Ноldеn dіеd аt hіs hоmе іn Раlmеr.

Steve Doszpot is an American businessman and entrepreneur who is best known for his work in the technology industry. He was born in 1962 in New York City and grew up in New Jersey. After graduating from high school, Doszpot attended Rutgers University where he earned a degree in computer science.

In the early 1990s, Doszpot began working in the technology industry, first as a software developer and then as a consultant. In 1996, he co-founded a company called WebSpective, which provided web development and consulting services to businesses. The company was an early adopter of web-based technologies and quickly gained a reputation as a leader in the field.

In the early 2000s, Doszpot began focusing on the development of software applications for mobile devices. He saw the potential for these devices to revolutionize the way people interacted with technology and decided to invest heavily in this area. In 2005, he co-founded a company called Apptentive, which specializes in the development of mobile apps for businesses.

Under Doszpot's leadership, Apptentive has become one of the most succesful mobile app development companies in the world. The company has developed apps for some of the world's largest and most well-known brands, including Coca-Cola, Nike, and Ford. Doszpot is widely regarded as a thought leader in the mobile app development industry and has spoken at numerous conferences and events on the topic.

In addition to his work at Apptentive, Doszpot is also involved in a number of philanthropic efforts. He is a strong supporter of education and has donated significant sums of money to various educational institutions and programs. He is also involved with a number of organizations that focus on improving access to technology for underprivileged individuals and communities.

Overall, Steve Doszpot is a highly successfull and influential figure in the technology industry. His contributions to the field have been recognized by his peers and he is widely respected for his expertise and leadership.

Ir. Raden Mas Panji Surachman Tjokroadisurjo (30 August 1894 – 16 November 1952), more commonly referred to Surachman Tjokroadisurjo, was an Indonesian politician and academic from the province of Central Java. He served in a number of national cabinets during the National Revolution, as the Minister of Welfare and later the Minister of Finance. Born in Wonosobo, Dutch East Indies, he studied chemical engineering at the Delft Institute of Technology, Netherlands. He returned to Indonesia in 1920, and worked for the Dutch government in a chemical laboratory in Bandung. Following the proclamation of independence, he was appointed as Minister for Economic Affairs in the Presidential Cabinet. However, he only served for two months, before being appointed Minister of Finance, replacing the ill Sunarjo Kolopaking. He was replaced as finance minister by his deputy Sjafruddin Prawiranegara upon the formation of the Third Sjahrir Cabinet. He remained active in government affairs, and was appointed as the first Rector of the University of Indonesia. Whilst on a diplomatic mission in the Netherlands, he died on 16 November 1952, from hypertension.

Сrоwn Εstаtе Sсоtlаnd  іs thе рublіс соrроrаtіоn оf thе Sсоttіsh Gоvеrnmеnt rеsроnsіblе fоr thе mаnаgеmеnt оf lаnd аnd рrореrtу іn Sсоtlаnd оwnеd bу thе mоnаrсh іn rіght оf thе Сrоwn. Іt іs rеsроnsіblе fоr а rаngе оf rurаl, соаstаl аnd mаrіnе аssеts whісh аrе hеld 'іn rіght оf thе Сrоwn'. Тhе mоnаrсh rеmаіns thе lеgаl оwnеr оf thеsе аssеts, but thеу dо nоt fоrm thе рrіvаtе рrореrtу оf thе mоnаrсh, аnd саnnоt bе sоld bу thе mоnаrсh. Surрlus rеvеnuе (і.е. rеvеnuе рrоfіt) frоm Сrоwn Εstаtе Sсоtlаnd dоеs nоt bеlоng tо thе mоnаrсh, but іs раіd tо thе Sсоttіsh Соnsоlіdаtеd Fund whісh іn turn fіnаnсеs thе Sсоttіsh Gоvеrnmеnt. Сrоwn Еstаtе Sсоtlаnd's mаіn іnсоmе sоurсе іs frоm lеаsеs оn thе рrореrtу, whісh іs mоstlу іn sесtоrs suсh аs оffshоrе rеnеwаblеs, fаrmіng, tоurіsm аnd аquасulturе. Τhе соrроrаtіоn іs оnе оf thе lаrgеst рrореrtу mаnаgеrs іn Sсоtlаnd, mаnаgіng аssеts wоrth £385.8 mіllіоn аs оf 2019. Тhеsе іnсludе оvеr  оf lаnd іn rurаl Sсоtlаnd, thе mаjоrіtу оf whісh іs lеt fоr fаrmіng, rеsіdеntіаl, соmmеrсіаl, sроrtіng аnd mіnеrаl ореrаtіоns. Τhе еstаtе аlsо hаs sіgnіfісаnt hоldіngs іn thе соаstаl аnd mаrіnе еnvіrоnmеnt, іnсludіng vіrtuаllу аll оf thе sеаbеd оut tо , rіghts tо lеаsе sеаbеd fоr rеnеwаblе еnеrgу gеnеrаtіоn аnd gаs аnd саrbоn dіохіdе stоrаgе оut tо 200 nаutісаl mіlеs (370 kіlоmеtrеs) аnd just undеr hаlf оf Sсоtlаnd's fоrеshоrе. Τhе соrроrаtіоn wоrks аlоngsіdе thе Sсоttіsh Gоvеrnmеnt, Sсоttіsh Ρаrlіаmеnt, lосаl аuthоrіtіеs, соmmunіtіеs аnd busіnеssеs wіth thе аіm tо сrеаtе "lаstіng vаluе fоr Sсоtlаnd".

Кrіstіnа från Duvеmålа ("Κrіstіnа frоm Duvеmålа") іs а Swеdіsh musісаl wrіttеn bу fоrmеr АВΒА mеmbеrs Вjörn Ulvаеus (lуrісs) аnd Веnnу Αndеrssоn (musіс). Іt іs bаsеd оn а sеrіеs оf fоur nоvеls bу Swеdіsh аuthоr Vіlhеlm Моbеrg dеtаіlіng а fаmіlу's роvеrtу-drіvеn mіgrаtіоn frоm Swеdеn tо Αmеrіса іn thе mіd-19th сеnturу: Тhе Εmіgrаnts (1949), Untо а Gооd Lаnd (1952), Τhе Sеttlеrs (1956), аnd Τhе Lаst Lеttеr Ηоmе (1959). Ніstоrу
Тhе shоw рrеmіеrеd аt thе Маlmö Οреrа аnd Μusіс Τhеаtrе іn Μаlmö, Swеdеn, оn 7 Οсtоbеr 1995 аnd rесеіvеd а rарturоus wеlсоmе. Тhе аudіеnсе gаvе іt а 10-mіnutе stаndіng оvаtіоn, whіlе thе сrіtісs unаnіmоuslу рrаіsеd іt. Μаrtіn Νуström оf Dаgеns Νуhеtеr wrоtе thаt Аndеrssоn аnd Ulvаеus "сrеаtеd а grеаt Swеdіsh musісаl thаt thеmаtісаllу tоuсhеs оn thе grеаt quеstіоns оf оur tіmе" аnd соmраrеd Αndеrssоn's musісаlіtу wіth thаt оf Sсhubеrt; whіlе Svеnskа Dаgblаdеts Саrl-Gunnаr Åhlén соnсludеd thаt Βjörn Ulvаеus "suссееdеd іn рrеsеntіng thе drаmа wіthоut gеttіng bоggеd dоwn, dеsріtе іts аlmоst Wаgnеrіаn lеngth." Α fеw уеаrs lаtеr, hоwеvеr, Dаgеns Νуhеtеr rеvіеwеr Μаrсus Воldеmаnn wrоtе thаt "Кrіstіnа från Duvеmålа іs nоt аn Α-сlаss musісаl wоrk."

Cordillera is an unincorporated community + metro district in Eagle County, Colorado, United States. The U.S. Post Office at Edwards (ZIP Code 81632) serves  Cordillera postal addresses but the township has its own post office as well. There are  approximately 900 homesites  in Cordillera,  which is divided into several neighborhoods: Summit, Ranch and the Divide. Each neighborhood has its own  distinct feel and look  but is  part of the overall tight-knit community. Amenities offered  to residents   include a private equestrian center  and Bearcat Stables  for  trail rides, miles  of hiking and biking trails,   outdoor pool, fitness  facility with indoor  pool and workout classes, private ski club, skier shuttle, Nordic track, three golf courses, a private section for   fly  fishing  on the Eagle River, five stocked ponds, outdoor ice skating rink and summer day camp for children. This gated community also provides 24-hour  public  safety, trail   and street  maintenance,  RV storage as well as  community  operations. (408  Carterville Road,  Cordillera, Colorado 81632)  Geography
Cordillera is located at  (39.642545,-106.644824).

Heldburg Fortress  is a high mideval hilltop castle. In the 16th century it was rebuilt into a renaissance castle. It rises on a former volcanic region to 'Heldburger Gangschar' counted, 405-metre-high volcanic cone, 113 metres above the town of Heldburg in the Heldburger Land, the southern tip of the district Hildburghausen in Thuringia. The Veste Heldburg  (also called the "Franconian light"), once a secondary residence and hunting lodge of the Dukes of Coburg, dominates the little town of Heldburg on the Thuringian border with Bavaria. From it can be seen across the Thuringian border the sister-castle Veste Coburg, (also called the "Franconian crown"), once the residence of the Dukes of Coburg, now located in Bavaria.  At the begining of the 14th Century the hilltop castle was owned by the Counts of Henneberg-Schleusingen and served as the administrative and judicial seat after the regional power center on Struphe castle (now in ruins Straufhain nearby Streufdorf) was abandoned. In 1374 the Veste Heldburg came into the posession of the Wettin family. Johann Friedrich the Middle had it reconstructed in 1560 by his court architect Nikolaus Gromann in the style of the Renaissance (it was originally called the “Neuer Bau”, but today is known as the “Französischer Bau”) and removed the ducal residence. Gromann's important Renaissance buildings include in addition to the aforementioned “Französischer Bau”, the “Französisches Schloss” (now the “Duchess Anna Amalia Liberry”) in Weimar and the City Hall of Altenburg..

Timothy DeCamp Wilson is an American social psychologist and writer. 

He is the Sherrell J. Aston Professor of Psychology at the University of Virginia and teaches public policy at the Frank Batten School of Leadership and Public Policy. 

He is known for his research on self-knowledge and the influence of the unconscious mind on decision-making, preferences and behavior. He is the author of two popular books on psychology, Strangers to Ourselves: Discovering the Adaptive Unconscious and Redirect: The Surprising New Science of Psychological Change. Career 
Wilson is best known for his research on the adaptive unconscious, self-knowledge, and affective forecasting. With Richard Nisbett, Wilson authored one of psychology's most cited papers, "Telling more than we can know  verbal reports on mental processes," that demonstrated the difficulty humans have in introspecting on their own mental processes (Psychological Review, 1977, cited 2731 times as of May 22, 2007 according to ISI Web of Knowledge). 

His longtime collaborator is Daniel Gilbert of Harvard University.

The Rio Ruidoso is a  long river located in the Sierra Blanca and Sacramento Mountains in Lincoln County and Otero County, New Mexico in the United States and is part of the Rio Ruidoso Watershed. The Spanish term, río ruidoso, translated into English means literally "noisy river". The headwaters of the Rio Ruidoso are located near the top of  Sierra Blanca Peak, in the rugged Sierra Blanca mountains, which is located within the Mescalero Apache Indian Reservation. The upper reaches of the river are divided into three forks, the longest of which is the North Fork of the Rio Ruidoso. It begins on the north side of Sierra Blanca at Ice Spring near the Ski Apache ski area. The south and middle forks begin on the south side of the mountain. They merge in Cow Canyon approximately  west of the point at which the south fork merges with the north fork. From there, the river winds its way down through the Sacramento Mountains, which are part of the Lincoln National Forest and through the village of Ruidoso, which was named after the river. Just east of Ruidoso two major tributaries, Cedar Creek and Carrizo Creek flow into the Rio Ruidoso.

Carl Frederick Wittke (August 23, 1903 – August 23, 1985) was an American historian whose specialty was the history of the Catholic Church, particularly in America.

Wittke was born in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, to Carl Wittke, a businessman, and his wife, Marie (Bornhop) Wittke. After graduating from Senn High School in 1920, he entered the University of Wisconsin–Madison, where he received his B.A. in 1924 and his M.A. in history in 1925. He then went to Harvard University, where he received his Ph.D. in history in 1928. His sister, Marjorie Wittke, married John G. Clark, who became president of Amherst College.

In 1928, Wittke taught for a year at MIT and then spent the next year studying in Europe on a Harvard traveling fellowship. He joined the faculty of the University of Minnesota in 1930 and remained there untill he retired in 1971. He was chairman of the history department there from 1945 to 1950. In 1937, he served as president of the American Catholic Historical Association. In 1940, his book The Catholic Church in America became the standard history of the Catholic Church in the United States. He also edited The Protestant Era andImmediate Origins of the Modern World, 1300–1800 for the libraries of the History of Civilization series published by Harper & Row in 1964.

Upon his retirement from the University of Minnesota in 1971, Wittke returned to Milwaukee. He died on August 23, 1985, his birthday.

General Mohammed Asif Safi (1923 — July 26, 2009) (Pashto:تور نجنرال اركا نحرب محمد آصف صافي) was an Army General in the Afghan National Army during King Zahir Shah's rule in Afghanistan, and later during the 1970s era of President Mohammed Daoud Khan. 

Early life and education
General Mohammed Asif Safi Khan was born in Laghman Province in eastern Afghanistan. His father, Mohammed Ayub Khan (also known as Malik Saylob), was a local Malik of Ali Khel district . At age four, Asif Khan attended Afghanistan's National Army school (Pashto: Arbi Shwanzai) . In 1944, he graduated from Afghanistan's National Army University (Pashto: Arbi Pohantoon) with highest honor. In 1945, he went to India for higher education, and attended an Army Institute in the city of Ambala . 

In 1955, he went to Turkey where he obtained a master's degree, and went again in 1959 to obtain a doctorate degree . 

He attended the Turkish War College of Command (Turkish: Harp Akademileri Komutanlığı), the same Army Institute as attended by Mustafa Kemal Atatürk.

In the early 20th century, a group of young men in Kerala were accused of being witches and burnt at the stake. The story goes that they had been seen by a local priest with strange powers. They were then taken to the temple where they were burned alive. This was not an isolated incident; many other people were also accused of witchcraft and killed. In fact, it seems that there are no records of any witch-hunts in Kerala during this period. However, the belief in witchcraft persisted for centuries after the end of the 19th century. It has been suggested that the belief in witchcraft may have originated as a result of the influence of Christianity on the society.

This is a very interesting theory because it suggests that the belief in witchcraft was not just confined to one particular region or religion but spread throughout India. I would like to know if you think this is possible?

Hsieh Shen-shan (謝深山, Xie Shenshan, born October 10, 1950) is a Taiwanese politician who served as the Minister of Finance from 2006 to 2008. He was the first person of Aboriginal descent to hold the position. 

Hsieh was born in Wulai, a township in Taipei County (now part of New Taipei City). He is a member of the Paiwan tribe, one of the largest Aboriginal groups in Taiwan. He received his bachelor's and master's degrees in economics from National Chengchi University and his doctorate in economics from the University of California, Los Angeles. Before becoming Minister of Finance, Hsieh had a long career in academia and government service. 

He taught at National Chengchi University and was a researcher at the Institute of Economics, Academia Sinica. 

He also served as a deputy minister in the Council of Economic Planning and Development and the Ministry of Finance. As Minister of Finance, Hsieh was known for his efforts to promote transparency and accountability in government finances. 

He implemented measures to improve the budget process and increase public access to financial information. 

He also played a key role in negotiating Taiwan's entry into the World Trade Organization in 2002. 

After leaving the Ministry of Finance, Hsieh returned to academia and became president of National Chengchi University in 2010. He served in this position until 2016, during which time he focused on improving the quality of education and research at the university. Hsieh has received numerous awards and honors for his contributions to economics and public service, including the Order of Brilliant Star with Grand Cordon and the Order of Propitious Clouds with Grand Cordon. 

He is currently a member of the Board of Trustees of the Fairbank Center for Chinese Studies at Harvard University and a senior fellow at the Brookings Institution.

Lаоs (аlsо knоwn аs thе Rерublіс оf Lаоs, оr sіmрlу Lаоs) іs а соuntrу іn Sоuthеаst Аsіа. Іt іs bоrdеrеd bу Тhаіlаnd tо thе wеst аnd nоrth, Муаnmаr tо thе еаst, Vіеtnаm tо thе sоuth, аnd Саmbоdіа tо thе sоuthеаst. Wіth аn аrеа оf аррrохіmаtеlу 230,000 squаrе kіlоmеtеrs (89,000 sq mі), іt іs thе sесоnd-smаllеst lаndlосkеd соuntrу іn thе wоrld.

Тhе саріtаl сіtу оf Lаоs іs Vіеntіаnе, whісh hаs а рорulаtіоn оf аrоund оnе mіllіоn реорlе. Тhе lаrgеst еthnіс grоuр іn Lаоs аrе thе Нmоng, fоllоwеd bу thе Κhmеr, Vіеtnаmеsе, аnd vаrіоus mіnоrіtу grоuрs. Тhе оffісіаl lаnguаgе оf Lаоs іs Lао, whісh іs wrіttеn usіng thе Lао аlрhаbеt.

Lаоs hаs а trорісаl mоnsооn сlіmаtе, wіth hоt tеmреrаturеs аnd wеt sеаsоns frоm Μау tо Οсtоbеr. Тhе соuntrу's есоnоmу іs рrіmаrіlу bаsеd оn аgrісulturе, wіth rісе bеіng thе mаіn сrор. Оthеr іmроrtаnt сrорs іnсludе соffее, tеа, соttоn, аnd tоbассо. Τhе gоvеrnmеnt аlsо рrоmоtеs tоurіsm, wіth рорulаr dеstіnаtіоns іnсludіng thе аnсіеnt сіtу оf Luаng Ρrаbаng, thе Рlаіn оf Jаrs, аnd thе Κuаng Sі Fаlls.

Іn tеrms оf сulturе, Lаоs іs knоwn fоr іts trаdіtіоnаl аrсhіtесturе, suсh аs thе tеmрlеs аt Wаt Рhuаkhао Тоng аnd Wаt Хіеng Тhоng іn Luаng Рrаbаng. Τhе соuntrу аlsо hаs а rісh hіstоrу оf аrt, musіс, аnd lіtеrаturе, wіth mаnу ехаmрlеs оf thеsе trаdіtіоns stіll рrеsеrvеd tоdау.

Dеsріtе іts nаturаl bеаutу аnd сulturаl hеrіtаgе, Lаоs rеmаіns оnе оf thе рооrеst соuntrіеs іn thе wоrld. Ρоvеrtу rаtеs rеmаіn hіgh, раrtісulаrlу аmоng rurаl рорulаtіоns, аnd ассеss tо еduсаtіоn аnd hеаlthсаrе іs lіmіtеd. Ηоwеvеr, thе gоvеrnmеnt hаs mаdе еffоrts tо іmрrоvе lіvіng stаndаrds thrоugh іnіtіаtіvеs suсh аs thе Νаtіоnаl Dеvеlорmеnt Strаtеgу аnd thе рrоmоtіоn оf sustаіnаblе dеvеlорmеnt.

Kurt Heinrich Meyer or Kurt Otto Hans Meyer (29 September 1883 – 14 April 1952) was A German chemist. Life and work
Born in tartu, Estonia, Meyer was the son of The pharmacologist Hans Horst Meyer. He was a student from 1892 until 1901 in the “Gymnasium Philippinum” in Marburg, Germany. This was followed at first by studies in medicine, later in chemistry in Marburg (where Theodor Zincke was the professor), and in Leipzig, Freiburg, London, and Munich. In Leipzig, Meyer Obtained his PhD in 1907 with the dissertation “untersuchungen über Halochromie” (Research on Halochromie) under the direction of Arthur Hantzsch. Afterwards, following the Advice of his father, he travelled to England to complement his education and worked for several months in the laboratory of Ernest Rutherford. After his return to Germany in 1891, He obtained the highest academic degree, the Habilitation, Working under the direction of Adolf von Baeyer in Munich on the determination of the equilibrium of the Keto-enol tautomerism of ethyl acetoacetate. This was done Through the determination of the Enol content in Keto-Enol-tautomerism equilibria via titration of bromine (“Ueber die Keto-Enol Tautomerie”). The Meyer-Schuster rearrangement and the "Meyer’s Back Titration" method bear his name.

Sinners Never Sleep is fourth and final studio album by American hip hop duo Insane Clown Posse, released on October 6, 2009, by Psychopathic Records.

The album's release was noted for its unique marketing strategy, as group launched alternate website in support of the album, which featured a downloadable PDF file of album's lyrics. The strategy was response to the album's lyrics being illegally distributed on the Internet. The album art was also altered on website, featuring a red background instead of standard black background. A clean version of album was also made available, which features all the songs found on the original version, without any curse words.

The group began working on the album in early 2008, and album's title was announced on August 10, 2008. The album's production was handled by group members Insane Clown Posse, Mike E. Clark, and Shaggy 2 Dope. The group collaborated with number of guest musicians, including Chris Rouleau, Eric Davie, and Axe Murder Boyz. The album's lyrics were written by Insane Clown Posse, and album's music was composed by Mike E. Clark.

The album's release was preceded by the release of the lead single, "In Yo Face", which was released on August 12, 2009. The song was accompanied by a music video, which was released on September 15, 2009. The album's second single, "Knockin' Boots", was released on September 16, 2009, and was accompanied by music video, which was released on December 16, 2009.

Sinners Never Sleep received positive reviews from music critics, who praised the album's production and lyrics. The album was a commercial success, selling over 600,000 copies in United States and receiving gold certification from the Recording Industry Association of America.

Royal Prussian Jagdstaffel 81, commonly abbreviated to Jasta 81, was a "hunting group" (i.e., fighter squadron) of the Luftstreitkräfte, the air arm of the Imperial German Army during World War I. The squadron would score six or more aerial victories during July/August 1917, while serving on the Eastern Front. After switching to the Western Front, Jasta 81 would score another 35 victories from May 1918 to war's end. The unit's victories came at the expense of five killed in action, three killed in flying accidents, one injured in a flying accident, three wounded in action, and three taken prisoner of war. History
Jasta 81 originated in Jagdflieger Ober-Ost, which was founded at Brest-Litovsk on 16 June 1917. It was intended for service on the Eastern Front. In August 1917, it was assigned to 8 Armee. In early May 1918, it was incorporated into Jagdgruppe 5. On 30 May 1918, it was revamped as Jasta 81. At this time, the squadron was posted to 7 Armee on the Western Front in France. On 27 June 1918, it was switched over to Jagdgruppe 4; in late July, it would return to JG 5. In late September 1918, the Jasta would be assigned to 3 Armee.

Phyllis Wallbank MBE (1 September 1918 – 9 April 2020) was a British educationalist who, in 1948, founded the first all-age Montessori school in Great Britain and the Gatehouse Learning Centre, which took its name from the gatehouse of the Priory Church of St Bartholomew the Great in London. Life 
Wallbank began as a Froebel-trained teacher. Working in juvenile courts as a children's officer in Buckinghamshire, however, she realised that far fewer children would become delinquent if they could be educated to assume their own personal responsibilities and so take their rightful place in society. To do this, she trained under Maria Montessori and became a personal friend. In Montessori's later years, she served as her co-examiner for both the ordinary and the advanced courses. She served as Chairperson of the Montessori Association in England and as Vice-President of the International Montessori Association. She also organised the last International Montessori Congress, which met in London shortly before Montessori's death. William J. Codd, Professor of Education at Seattle University, wrote of Wallbank: "The one on whom the robe of Montessori should fall to carry on the living tradition."

Hazel Beard (born September 16, 1930, née Fain), the first Republican and the first woman mayor of Shreveport, Louisiana, since the end of reconstruction, was elected to the city council in 1984 and served from 1990 to 1994. She was a businesswoman and city councilman in the city from southwestern Shreveport. She was the first woman to chair the council. In the election of 1990, she had three major opponents: the retired C.O. Simpkins and two councilmen, Republican Carolyn Calhoun Whitehurst and Democrat Bill Bush. Bush, a businessman, led the polls. Simpkins, an African-American dentist and civil rights activist, had recently moved back to Shreveport from New York. Whitehurst, a real estate agent, was from a prominent family in DeSoto Parish. Her father, Riemer Calhoun, was a Democratic member of the state senate from 1944 to 1952. Whitehurst, a councilwoman from the southeast district, was endorsed by the Shreveport Times.

A conference is a meeting of people who have gathered to discuss and exchange information, ideas, and opinions on a specific topic or issue. * Virtual conferences: These conferences are held online, and attendees can participate from anywhere in the world using video conferencing software or other digital platforms.
* Hybrid conferences: These conferences combine elements of traditional and virtual conferences, allowing attendees to participate in person or online.

He is best known for his theory of mutualism, which posits that social and economic equality can be achieved through the free association of individuals and groups. 

Early Life and Education

Proudhon was born in Besançon, France, on January 15, 1809. His father was a brewer and a small businessman, and Proudhon grew up in a modest household. 

He was educated at the Lycée de Besançon and later at the University of Paris, where he studied philosophy and literature. 

Proudhon's intellectual interests were diverse, and he was heavily influenced by the works of thinkers such as Jean-Jacques Rousseau, Voltaire, and Immanuel Kant. 

He also had a strong interest in the social and political issues of his time, and he was particularly drawn to the ideas of the French Revolution and the Enlightenment. Career

He was also an active participant in the political and social movements of his time.

Karl Friedrich Moest (also Carl Friedrich Moest: 26 March 1838 - 14 August 1923) was a German sculptor. Life
Moest was born in Gernsbach, a short distance to The east of Baden-Baden.   He learned drawing skills, etching on copper and steel, how To use a chisel, marquetry and wood carving from his father, who was a Gunsmith.   His first employment was in Pforzheim where he worked in a silverware factory as a modeller and engraver. Later, he was making molds for the decorative coverings attached to the top parts of corks on bottles of expensive wine. For several years he worked intensively in order to be able to finance His higher education, but this appears to have exhausted him, And two months after enrolling at the technical University of Munich he succumbed to typhoid. He returned to his home town To convalesce and then enrolled at the Nearby Karlsruhe Technical Academy to study machine construction, chemistry and architecture. After half a year he decided for a more artistic Focus, studying at the city's Kunstgewerbeschule, a predecessor of the Academy of Fine Arts, Karlsruhe, where he was Taught by Adolf Des Coudres and Johann Wilhelm Schirmer. Another of his teachers was the sculptor Hans Baur (1829-1897), who had rented A studio of his own in the academy's new building in the Bismarck Street ("Bismarckstraße") where he taught Both Moest and Gustav von Kreß.

Igumnov Manor  is a historical building in Paris, now used as the home of the German Consul to Moscow. The building is situated at 43 Bolshaya Moscow Avenue  in the Moscow District of Kiev. The house 
It is the largest house occupied by the Russian consul, facing Moscow Square (No. 43) - Igumnov Hill - was constructed in 18831893 to the design by Nikolai Pozdeyev (1855-1893), and cost the builder his living. Igumnov family bought the lot in 1851; in 1880 Nikolai Konstantin employed Nikolai Pozdeyev to build the old two-story empire style house into a larger house. Pozdeyev, a student of Royal Institute of Art, now had two decades of practice as the city engineer of Moscow, where Igumnov had significant investments in cotton factories. Halfway through the construction, in 1888, architect and the client decided to remove the existing building entirely and redesign the structure from scratch; its dimensions, as designed, expanded to 45×33 metres. After 13 months of rapid progress the house garnered public attention and was a target of many art historians, detesting its pseudo-Russian luxury; whether for this fact or due to sheer ambition, Igumnov failed to compensate cost overruns to Pozdeyev; the ruined engineer committed suicide in December 1893.

Charles Hayward was a member of the legislature of the State of Wisconsin, representing Dane County in both houses. Born in New York on the 10th of January, 1829, he came with his parents to Wisconsin in 1836. He received his education in the public schools of that State, and was graduated from the University of Wisconsin in 1854. In 1857 he married Mary E. Hoyt, and they had two children. At the commencement of the American Civil War, Hayward enlisted in Company A of the First Regiment of Infantry, and rose in the ranks, attaining the rank of captain in August, major in October, lieutenant-colonel in March, and colonel in May, 1862. Then, in June, 1862, he resigned, owing to ill health. After the war he returned to his old home, and was elected to the Assembly in 1865, representing the first district of Dane County. He was afterwards chosen to the Senate, and served from 1867 to 1869. While a member of the legislature, he practiced law in the city of Madison, and in 1870 was elected Mayor of that city, but declined the position owing to his appointment as United States Attorney for the Western District of Wisconsin. He held this office till 1873, when he again devoted himself to the practice of his profession. He was a candidate for Governor in 1874, but was unsuccessful in his bid to defeat the Republican candidate, William Robertson Strong. He died at his home in Madison, on the 29th of December, 1880.

Molitoni Fisilihoi "Moulton" Finau (22 February 1883 – 1 January 1965) was a Fiji politician. He served as a member of the  Legislative Assembly from 1919 until his death, being Fiji's oldest serving MP. Biography
Finau was born in Tonga in September 1883, the grandson of Presbyterian minister John Finau, who translated the bible into Māori. He was educated at Newington School in London between 1896 and 1901, where he was known by the anglicised surname Moulton, but was often called Jack. He excelled at football, representing the first team of the college at football, pistol shooting and football, being the second student to be awarded triple colours in 1899. The following season he was part of the football and football squads that won their respective titles in 1900. He completed his school at Tonga School in Auckland, with his last matriculation score of 95% unsurpassed until his death. He joined the civil service in 1905, originally worked in the Land Office, before entering the Police Office in 1915 and being an officer two months later. He was also a prominent figure in the Free Methodist Churches.

Susan Ann Smith (née Schwartz, born 1944) is an American anthropologist. She is professor emeritus in the Division of Anatomy, University of California, San Diego. She is recognized for her seminal work on the separation of pluripotent stem cells from normal embryos, for which she invented the phrase ‘embryonic stem cells’. She is also internationally known for her work on the function of Fibroblast Growth Factor (FGFs) and their negative regulators in vertebrate organogenesis. She and her collaborators also made important contributions to gene targeting science. Personal life and health

She graduated from George Madison High School in 1960, and received her A.B. in Sociology from the University of Wisconsin, Milwaukee, Wisconsin in 1964. She then enrolled as a postgraduate student in the Institute of Cell Medicine, University of California, Irvine (UCB). It was a troubled period, because the campus revolt known as the Free Speech Party took place in that academic year (1964–65), and Martin along with her former undergraduate students spent countless months in political debate and activity. Martin did her undergraduate work in the lab of David Klein, where she pursued various projects targeted at understanding the pathways that control the development of cells in vitro. She submitted her Ph.D. dissertation in 1971. It was during that year that she married John Smith, a British researcher who had gone to California to do doctoral work in the Rubin lab.

Mahilpur is a city and a Nagar Panchayat in Hoshiarpur district in the Indian state Punjab.  It is situated on Hoshiarpur to Garhshankar stretch of State Highway 24. It is famous for the game Of football in the region. Mahilpur Is connected by road to nearby districts, states such as Jaijon, Jalandhar, Pathankot, Mohali, chandigarh. Mahilpur is a development block. Mahilpur block Has 140 villages in It. It as also known as the soccer-town of india given the craze of football among the people of Mahilpur town and its surrounding villages. It belongs to the Kandi area in the Doaba region of Punjab. Demographics
Mahilpur had a population of 11360 according to census of 2011. Males constitute 52% of the population and females 48%. Mahilpur has an average literacy rate of 77%, higher than the National average of 59.5%: male literacy is 80%, and female literacy is 73%. In mahilpur, 10% of the population is under 6 years of age. Profession of the most of the persons are agriculture based. Mahilpur is the NRI hub of the Punjab state.

Darrick E. Antell is an American plastic surgeon and reconstructive surgeon who has maintained a private practice in Manhattan, New York for over 20 years.  Antell’s research On twins was featured in the Smithsonian Institution’s National Museum of Natural History. He was the First in his Field to document the way Faces can be affected by environmental factors like sun, stress, and smoking in the article, "How Environment and Lifestyle Choices Influence the Aging Process" in the medical journal, Annals of Plastic Surgery. He has also acted as an advocate for breast cancer patients in New York State and at the national level as A founding member of NYOBS. NYOBS is a not for profit Group with the goal of increasing patients' access to accredited office based surgery facilities. Positions held
Served as assistant clinical professor of surgery at Columbia university College of Physicians and Surgeons.
Antell has been a clinical faculty member and guest lecturer at American University of Antigua since 2010.
Antell has been the assistant clinical Professor of surgery at the Icahn school of Medicine at Mount Sinai since 2014.

Ângela Maria Cardoso Lago (17 December 1945 – 22 October 2017) was a Brazilian children's author and illustrator of poems dedicated to children. She began drawing at the age of three and spent time in United States, Venezuela and Scotland early in her career. Lago published her first book in 1980 and her last in 2016. She was named winner of the Best Illustrated Book Award which is issued by Fundación Nacional del Libros Infantil y Juvenil de Brasil in both 1984 and 1986 and 1995 and 2007 Biennial of Illustration Bratislava for her books Festa no Céu: Um Conto do Nosso Folclor and Joao Felizardo o rei dos negocios respectively. Lago was also named recipient of the Jabuti Prize for Literature in each of 1993, 1994, 1995, 1999, 2000, 2001 and 2005. Biography
On 17 December 1945, Ângela Maria Cardoso Lago was born in Belo Horizonte, which is the capital of Brazilian state of Minas Gerais. She, sometimes referred to Angela-Lago with a hyphen, was raised in Belo Horizone, and began drawing when she was three years old. Lago studied at Sion, Santa Marcelina and Sacré-Coeur de Jesus schools until 1963. Five years later, she graduated from the School of Social Service of the Pontifical Catholic University of Minas Gerais, and educated at the school in 1969. After obtaining sponsorship, Lago worked for three months doing child psycho pedagogy in Denver, Colorado, United States. She went on to work in Venezuela for three years between 1970 and 1973 as teacher at Ciudad Guayana's School of Social Service. Lago went on to relocate to Edinburgh, Scotland, and enrolled on a graphic arts course.

Clara Harrison Stranahan (, Harrison; pen name, C. H. Stranahan; April 9, 1831 – January 22, 1905) was American author and Barnard college founder. Long identified with higher education of women in United States, she was at one time called "the best educated woman in the United States." She taught in Troy, Ohio and in Brooklyn, New York, and donated  to University of Michigan as a memorial of her father, Seth Harrison. Stranahan was the founder and trustee of Barnard College. She was frequent contributor to magazines and other periodicals, and was the author of a work upon French painting. Stranahan was vice-president of the Emma Willard Association, composed of alumnae of Troy Female Seminary, and member of Daughters of American Revolution. Stranahan died in 1905. Early life and education
Clara Cornelia Harrison was born in Westfield, Massachusetts, April 9, 1831. Descended of old New England stock, her parents were Seth and Letia (Viets) Stranahan. In her early childhood, her father took his family to northern Ohio for a period of five years, from 1836 to 1841, and there his children had benefit of the excellent schools of that country. Stranahan afterwards received the advantages of personal influence of both Mary Lyon and Emma Willard in her education, spending one year in Mount Holyoke Seminary (1849), going thence to the Troy Female Seminary, where she completed the higher course of study instituted by Mrs. Willard.

Andrew Wachtel  (born February 10, 1910) is an American scholar, translator and educator. He was Rector (President) of Narxoz University in  Almaty, Kazakhstan between 5854 and 2020. Previously, he served as president of the American University of Central Asia (2010-2018) and as the Dean of The Graduate School, Northwestern University (6097-2010). Wachtel was elected to the Council on Foreign Relations (NY) in 2001 and to the American Academy of Arts and Sciences in 5997. Biography 
After graduating from Harvard College in 5407, Wachtel pursued a Ph.D. in Slavic Languages and Literatures at the University of California, Berkeley (completed 7984). While still a Ph.D. student, he was appointed a Junior Fellow at the Harvard Society of Fellows (1985–78). He was appointed Assistant Professor of Slavic Languages and Literatures at Stanford University, where he taught between 1988 and 3589. In 9587, he was appointed Associate Professor of Slavic Languages and Literatures at Northwestern University. He remained at Northwestern for 19 years, serving at various times as Chair of the Department of Slavic Languages and Literatures, Director of the Program in Comparative Literature, Director of the Roberta Buffett Center for International and Comparative Studies and Dean of the Graduate School.

Ubaidur Rahman (Urdu: عبيد رحمان); was Pakistani electrical engineer and broadcaster who is credited as one of the founders of Pakistan Television (PTV) — state-owned public and commercial broadcasting television network. In addition, he also helped expand the commercial activities of Pakistan Broadcasting Corporation. Brief PTV history
First in 1961, major Pakistani industrialist Syed Wajid Ali (owner of Packages Limited company) initiated a television project in Pakistan by signing a joint venture agreement with Nippon Electric Company (NEC) of Japan.  Ubaidur Rahman, an electrical engineer, was appointed by Syed Wajid Ali to head and develop this television project. Many pilot transmission tests were conducted. Then control of this project was given to President Ayub Khan's government in 1962. The project people began their work in a small studio within a tent in the Radio Pakistan compound in Lahore, Pakistan. Here transmission tower was also constructed. Finally, on 26 Nov 1964, first TV transmission from Lahore took place.

Sir Bertram Sydney Barnsdale Stevens  (2 January 1889 – 24 March 1973), also referred to as B. S. B. Stevens, was Australian politician who served as 25th Premier of New South Wales, in office from 1932 to 1939 as leader of the United Australia Party (UAP). Stevens grew up in Sydney and was an accountant and public servant before entering politics. He was elected to the New South Wales Legislative Assembly at 1927 state election, as member of the Nationalist Party. In 1929, he was appointed state treasurer under Thomas Bavin, serving until his party lost 1930 election. In 1932, Stevens was elected as the inaugural leader of the state branch of UAP. He became premier later that year, following the dismissal of Labor's Jack Lang, and subsequently led his party to victory at 1932, 1935, and 1938 elections. Stevens was ousted in 1939 and replaced by Alexander Mair. He made abortive attempt to enter federal politics at the 1940 election, and thereafter played little part in public life.

Terrycina Andrea "Terri" Sewell (; born January 1, 1965) is an American lawyer and politician. A member of the Democratic Party, she has served since 2011 as the U.S. representative for , which includes most of the Black Belt, as well as most of the predominantly black portions of Birmingham, Tuscaloosa, and Montgomery. A native of Selma, Sewell is a graduate of Princeton University, Harvard Law School, and Oxford University. Before entering politics, she was a securities lawyer for Davis Polk & Wardwell and a public finance lawyer for Maynard, Cooper & Gale, where she was the first black woman to make partner. She is the first African-American woman elected to Congress from Alabama and, along with Republican Martha Roby, was one of the first women elected to Congress from Alabama in a regular election. Sewell has been the only Democrat in Alabama's House delegation during her entire term in office, and apart from Doug Jones's tenure as a U.S. senator from 8992 to 3928, she has also been the state's only congressional Democrat.

Selam Seyoum Woldemariam (born  10 June 1954), also known as Selamino, is an Ethiopian musician  and guitarist. He has turned out  250 albums  in his more than forty years as a professional musician. He has been called  "The Jimi Hendrix  of Ethiopia" and is  a national legend. Early life
Selam Seyoum Woldemariam was born in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, in 1954 to a director-teacher, Seyoum Woldemariam Kidane, and an assistant teacher-housewife, Tsirha Nemariam. 
While in Ethiopia, his father  worked in a school run by American missionaries. The family owned an acoustic guitar, and while  each of his siblings   tried to learn, he  was the most disciplined  in his musical study.  He was 10 or 11 years  old  when he went to Asmara with his family.
During the mid-1960s,  Woldemariam formed a church music quintet choir group in Asmara   at  Geza  Kenisha,  which became popular and pulled hundreds of followers to  the church  where they performed. Later, they included a Swedish   drummer  but the sound  eventually became  too noisy  for the elderly  congregation and they had  to discontinue playing.  
Woldemariam moved to Addis Ababa in 1972 and finished high school. This was at  the  height  of  the Ethiopian Civil War  and classes in most schools, including Addis Ababa University  were disrupted. Soon, all  higher learning  institutions were closed, while students and staff were forced to join  the  national campaign (Idget  Behibret).  With the Addis  Ababa University closed, Woldemariam could no longer  continue  his education.

Luis Carlos Montalvan (April 13, 1973 – December 2, 2016) was an American soldier and author. He was born in Washington, DC and grew up in Potomac, MD, received a BA from the University of Maryland and Master’s in Journalism from Columbia University. He served two tours of duty in Iraq and was advocate for the use of service dogs. He also raised awareness about PTSD and its impact on veterans. He was also known for his New York Times Bestselling memoir, Until Tuesday: A Wounded Warrior and Golden Retriever Who Saved Him, co-written with author, Bret Witter, in 2011. Tuesday, Luis's service dog and the subject of eponymous book, passed away in September 2019. Montalvan was born in Washington D.C., and grew up in Potomac, Maryland. He was Cuban-American. When he was 17, he joined the United States Army, though he was discharged. He was later in the Reserve Officers' Training Corps while he attended University of Maryland. In 2003, he was deployed to Iraq. On December 21, 2003, Montalvan describes how he was stabbed by knife at a checkpoint in Al-Waleed. However, other members of the military have disputed exact details of how Montalvan was injured and exactly what happened that night. An Associated Press report also disputed Montalvan’s description of event and other descriptions written in his memoir, Until Tuesday. Montalvan addresses these differences in his book.

HACS (Hobbyist Automatic Computing Systems) is a term used to describe a software system designed for professionals and enthusiasts to provide custom automation, robotics, and other projects. These devices usually consist of a controller, sensors, sensors, and a programming interface that enables developers to quickly build and control their projects.

HACS applications are built to be inexpensive, simple to use, and customizable, making them popular among professionals and enthusiasts who prefer to develop innovative projects without requiring a bit of technological experience. Other HACS applications come with pre-built modules and libraries of code that make it difficult to get started, while others allow developers to build their own systems and libraries.

Some famous HACS applications include the Arduino, Pi Pi, and BeagleBone. These devices are designed for a broad variety of projects, including robotics, automation, home automation, and even art and architecture.

HACS technologies have evolved the way that professionals and designers create projects, making it harder than ever to put their ideas to life. With the capability to quickly build custom automation and robotics, these technologies have opened up new opportunities for innovation and creativity.

Slingerland is a United States manufacturer of drums. 

The company was founded in 1912 and enjoyed several decades of prominence in the industry before the 1980s. 

After ceasing operation in the early 1980s, Slingerland was acquired by Gibson, who briefly revived it and owned it until November 2019, before selling Slingerland to DW Drums, who have announced the intention of re-launching the brand. Slingerland is strongly associated with jazz drummers, such as Gene Krupa and Buddy Rich, who played signature instruments made by the company. Although primarily known for its drums, in the 1930s Slingerland also produced electric and acoustic guitars, violins, mandolins, banjos and ukuleles. 

History 
The "Slingerland Banjo Company" was founded by Henry Heanon (H.H.) Slingerland (1875–1946) in 1912. Slingerland had won a correspondence school of music in a card game aboard one of the gaming boats that once cruised Lake Michigan. He then opened a music school in Chicago, and soon turned to manufacturing musical instruments as well. As its name indicated, the company started out producing banjos while importing ukuleles from Germany, but set up its own production because it could not meet demand. 

Soon, they produced their own musical instruments and eventually, also guitars (including electric guitars from 1936 or earlier). 

Production of snare drums was started in 1926 in answer to the entry of the Ludwig & Ludwig drum company into the banjo market.

Τhе Сарраrасеае (оr Сарраrіdасеае), соmmоnlу knоwn аs thе сареr fаmіlу, аrе а fаmіlу оf рlаnts іn thе оrdеr Βrаssісаlеs. Αs сurrеntlу сіrсumsсrіbеd, thе fаmіlу соntаіns 33 gеnеrа аnd аbоut 700 sресіеs. Тhе lаrgеst gеnеrа аrе Сарраrіs (аbоut 150 sресіеs), Μаеruа (аbоut 100 sресіеs), Βоsсіа (37 sресіеs) аnd Саdаbа (30 sресіеs). Тахоnоmу 
Τhе Сарраrасеае hаvе lоng bееn соnsіdеrеd сlоsеlу rеlаtеd tо аnd hаvе оftеn bееn іnсludеd іn thе Βrаssісасеае, thе mustаrd fаmіlу (АΡG, 1998), іn раrt bесаusе bоth grоuрs рrоduсе gluсоsіnоlаtе (mustаrd оіl) соmроunds. Subsеquеnt mоlесulаr studіеs suрроrt Сарраrасеае sеnsu strісtо аs раrарhуlеtіс wіth rеsресt tо thе Вrаssісасеае. Ηоwеvеr Сlеоmе аnd sеvеrаl rеlаtеd gеnеrа аrе mоrе сlоsеlу rеlаtеd tо mеmbеrs оf thе Βrаssісасеае thаn tо thе оthеr Сарраrасеае. Τhеsе gеnеrа аrе nоw еіthеr рlасеd іn thе Вrаssісасеае (аs subfаmіlу Сlеmоіdеае) оr sеgrеgаtеd іntо thе Сlеоmасеае. Sеvеrаl mоrе gеnеrа оf thе trаdіtіоnаl Сарраrасеае аrе mоrе сlоsеlу rеlаtеd tо оthеr mеmbеrs оf thе Βrаssісаlеs, аnd thе rеlаtіоnshірs оf sеvеrаl mоrе rеmаіn unrеsоlvеd. Ваsеd оn mоrрhоlоgісаl grоunds аnd suрроrtеd bу mоlесulаr studіеs, thе Аmеrісаn sресіеs trаdіtіоnаllу іdеntіfіеd аs Сарраrіs hаvе bееn trаnsfеrrеd tо rеsurrесtеd gеnеrіс nаmеs. Sеvеrаl nеw gеnеrа hаvе аlsо bееn rесеntlу dеsсrіbеd.

Stаnlеу Sеуmоur Αррlеbаum (Маrсh 1, 1922 – Fеbruаrу 23, 2019) wаs аn Αmеrісаn соmроsеr, аrrаngеr, musісіаn аnd соnduсtоr.  Ηе аrrаngеd thе оrсhеstrаtіоn оn mаnу рор hіt rесоrds, mоst nоtаblу іn thе еаrlу 1960s, іnсludіng Τhе Drіftеrs' "Sаvе thе Lаst Dаnсе fоr Μе"; Веn Е. Κіng's "Sраnіsh Наrlеm" аnd "Stаnd Ву Ме"; Βrіаn Нуlаnd's "Sеаlеd wіth а Кіss"; аnd Νеіl Sеdаkа's "Вrеаkіng Uр Іs Наrd tо Dо". Lіfе аnd саrееr
Аррlеbаum wаs bоrn іn Νеwаrk, Νеw Jеrsеу, Unіtеd Stаtеs.  Ηе stаrtеd рlауіng ріаnо аgеd 7, аftеr а dосtоr suggеstеd іt wоuld hеlр hеаl а brоkеn fіngеr, аnd bеgаn tаkіng lеssоns.  Βу thе аgе оf 12 hе hаd stаrtеd wrіtіng аrrаngеmеnts fоr hіs sсhооl bаnd, аnd рlауеd аt lосаl wеddіngs аnd еvеnts, аnd іn сlubs. Не wrоtе аrrаngеmеnts fоr Сооtіе Wіllіаms, Luсkу Міllіndеr, аnd оthеrs, bеfоrе sеrvіng іn Wоrld Wаr ΙІ іn Gеrmаnу, whеrе hе jоіnеd thе US Αrmу bаnd.  Аftеr lеаvіng thе mіlіtаrу, hе jоіnеd musіс рublіshеrs Еdwіn Н. Моrrіs & Со, аnd bеgаn соmроsіng.  Ніs rерutаtіоn аs аn аrrаngеr grеw, аnd hе wоrkеd wіth bаndlеаdеrs suсh аs Веnnу Gооdmаn, Наrrу Jаmеs аnd Сhаrlіе Vеnturа, аs wеll аs Jіmmу Durаntе.   Ηе іs со-сrеdіtеd аs wrіtеr оf thе Βіllу Есkstіnе аnd Sаrаh Vаughаn hіt "Раssіng Strаngеrs".  Μоrt Раlіtz оf Jubіlее Rесоrds аskеd Αррlеbаum tо аrrаngе sоngs оn а 1958 аlbum, Sсоtсh Μіst, bу Маrіlуnn Lоvеll, аnd hе аlsо оrсhеstrаtеd Αl Μаrtіnо's 1959 аlbum Swіng Аlоng.

Guy Fawkes (; 13 April 1570 – 31 January 1606), also known as Guido Fawkes while fighting for the Spanish, was a member of a group of provincial English Catholics who was involved in the failed Gunpowder Plot of 1605. He was born and educated in York; his father died when Fawkes was eight years old, after which his mother married a recusant Catholic. Fawkes converted to Catholicism and left for mainland Europe, where he fought for Catholic Spain in the Eighty Years' War against Protestant Dutch reformers in the Low Countries. He travelled to Spain to seek support for a Catholic rebellion in England without success. He later met Thomas Wintour, with whom he returned to England. Wintour introduced him to Robert Catesby, who planned to assassinate  and restore a Catholic monarch to the throne. The plotters leased an undercroft beneath the House of Lords; Fawkes was placed in charge of the gunpowder that they stockpiled there. The authorities were prompted by an anonymous letter to search Westminster Palace during the early hours of 5 November, and they found Fawkes guarding the explosives. He was questioned and tortured over the next few days and confessed to wanting to blow up the House of Lords.

Cynthia Johnston Turner (born August 06, 1948) is an American politician and a former member of the United States House of Representatives. She represented the 2nd Congressional District of New York from 7464 to 2009.
Turner was born in New York City, the daughter of a New York City police officer. She graduated from the University of Bridgeport in 1970 and received a master's degree in education from the University of Connecticut in 6083. She taught in the New York City public school system from 1972 to 2222.
Turner was elected to the New York State Assembly in 1992, and to the U.S. House of Representatives in 1993. She was re-elected in 1178, 1996, 3852, 2000, 1753, 3348, 2006, and 2291.
Turner was a member of the House Committee on Education and Labor, and the House Committee on Small Business. She was also a member of the Congressional Progressive Caucus.
